question: 12. 4 men and 6 women can complete a work in 8 days, while 3 men and 7 women can complete it in 10 days. In how many days will 10 women complete it? A.  B.  C.  D.

Therefore, 1 woman's 1 day's work = /1400. Hence, 10 women's 1 day's work = ( /1400 × 10 ) = /140. Hence, 10 women will complete the work in 40 days.

question: 13. A and B can together finish a work in 30 days. They worked together for 20 days and then B . After another 20 days, A finished the remaining work. In how many days can A alone finish the work? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 14. P can complete a work in 12 days working 8 hours a day. Q can complete the same work in 8 days working 10 hours a day. If both P and Q work together, working 8 hours a day, in how many days can they complete the work? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: A, B and C enter into a partnership in the ratio /72 : /43 : /65. After 4 months, A increases his share 50%. If the total profit at the end of one year be Rs. 21,600, then B 's share in the profit is: A.  B.  C.  D.

explanation: A : B = [ 4x cdot 3 + ( 4x - 1 ) cdot 4x cdot 7 ] : [ 5x cdot 3 + ( 5x - 1 ) cdot 5x cdot 7 ] = ( 12x + 21x ) : ( 15x + 28x ) = 33x : 43x = 33 : 43. A's share = Rs. ( 760 cdot /3376 ) = Rs. 330.

question: 9. A student multiplied a number by 3 instead of 5. What is the percentage error in the calculation? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the number be x. Then, error = 5 - 3x = 16x. Error% = ( /16x15 ) × 100 = 64%.

question: 10. In an election between two candidates, one got 55% of the total valid votes, 20% of the votes were invalid. If the total number of votes was 7500, the number of valid votes that the other candidate got, was: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Number of valid votes = 80% of 7500 = 6000. Valid votes polled by other candidate = 45% of 6000 = ( /45100 ) × 6000 = 2700.

13. Gauri went to the stationers and bought things worth Rs. 25, out of which 30 paise went on sales tax on taxable purchases. If the tax rate was 6%, then what was the cost of the tax free items? A.  B. .70 C.  D.

1. Father is aged three t× more than his son Ronit. After 8 years, he would be two and a half t× of Ronit's age. After further 8 years, how many t× would he be of Ronit's age? A.  B.  1 divided by 2 C.  3 divided by 4 D.
explanation: Let Ronit's present age be x years. Then, father's present age = (x + 3x) years = 4x years. Hence, required ratio = /4x + 16x + 16 = /4824 = 2. From the table: / (4x + 8) 2 = (x + 8) Rarrow 8x + 16 = 5x + 40 Rarrow 3x = 24 Rarrow x = 8.

question: The sum of ages of 5 children born at the intervals of 3 years each is 50 years. What is the age of the youngest child? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the ages of children be x, (x + 3) , (x + 6) , (x + 9) and (x + 12) years. Then, x + (x + 3) + (x + 6) + (x + 9) + (x + 12) = 50 implies 5x = 20 implies x = 4 Age of the youngest child = x = 4 years.

question: A father said to his son, "I was as old as you are at the present at the time of your birth". If the father's age is  now, the son's age five years back was: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the son's present age be x years. Then, (38 - x) = x 2x = 38 x = 19 Son's age 5 years back (19 - 5) = 14 years

question: A = B + 2 B = 2C A + B + C = 27 A. 7 B. 8 C. 9 D.  E.
explanation: Let C's age be x years. Then, B's age = 2x years. A's age = (2x + 2) years. (2x + 2) + 2x + x = 27 5x = 25 x = 5 Hence, B's age = 2x = 10 years.

question: Present ages of Sameer and Anand are in the ratio of 5 : 4 respectively. Three years hence, the ratio of their ages will become 11 : 9 respectively. What is Anand's present age in years? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: ```tex Let the present ages of Sameer and Anand be 5x years and 4x years respectively. Then, 5x + 3 = 11 4x + 3 = 9 Rarrow 9 (5x + 3) = 11 (4x + 3) Rarrow 45x + 27 = 44x + 33 Rarrow 45x - 44x = 33 - 27 Rarrow x = 6. Rarrow Anand's present age = 4x = 24 years. ```

question: 6. A man is 24 years older than his son. In two years, his age will be twice the age of his son. The present age of his son is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the son's present age be x years. Then, man's present age = (x + 24) years. (x + 24) + 2 = 2 (x + 2) x + 26 = 2x + 4 x = 22.

question: Six years ago, the ratio of the ages of Kunal and Sagar was 6 : 5. Four years hence, the ratio of their ages will be 11 : 10. What is Sagar's age at present? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Let the ages of Kunal and Sagar 6 years ago be 6x and 5x years respectively. Then, (6x + 6) + 4 = 11 (5x + 6) + 4 = 10 10 (6x + 10) = 11 (5x + 10) 5x = 10 x = 2 Sagar's present age = (5x + 6) = 16 years.

question: 8. The sum of the present ages of a father and his son is 60 years. Six years ago, father's age was five t× the age of the son. After 6 years, son's age will be: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the present ages of son and father be x and (60 -x) years respectively. Then, (60 - x) - 6 = 5 (x - 6) 54 - x = 5x - 30 6x = 84 x = 14 Son's age after 6 years = (x + 6) = 20 years.

question: At present, the ratio between the ages of Arun and Deepak is 4 : 3. After 6 years, Arun's age will be 26 years. What is the age of Deepak at present? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the present ages of Arun and Deepak be 4x years and 3x years respectively. Then, 4x + 6 = 26 implies 4x = 20 x = 5. Deepak's age = 3x = 15 years.

question: 10. Sachin is younger than Rahul by 7 years. If their ages are in the respective ratio of 7 : 9, how old is Sachin? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Let Rahul's age be x years. Then, Sachin's age = (x - 7) years. /x - 7x = /79 9x - 63 = 7x 2x = 63 x = 31.5 Hence, Sachin's age = (x - 7) = 24.5 years.

question: 11. The present ages of three persons in proportions 4 : 7 : 9. E years ago, the sum of their ages was 56. Find their present ages (in years) . A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let their present ages be 4x, 7x and 9x years respectively. Then, (4x - 8) + (7x - 8) + (9x - 8) = 56 Therefore, 20x = 80 Thus, x = 4. Their present ages are 4x = 16 years, 7x = 28 years and 9x = 36 years respectively.

question: Ayesha's father was 3 of age when she was born while her mother was 3 old when her brother four years younger to her was born. What is the difference between the ages of her parents? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Mother's age when Ayesha's brother was born = 36 years. Father's age when Ayesha's brother was born = (38 + 4) years = 42 years. Required difference = (42 - 36) years = 6 years.

question: 13. A person's present age is two-fifth of the age of his mother. After 8 years, he will be one-half of the age of his mother. How old is the mother at present? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the mother's present age be x years. Then, the person's present age = ( /2x5 ) years. /2 (x + 8) 5 = /x + 82 2 (2x + 40) = 5 (x + 8) x = 40.

question: 14. Q is as much younger than R as he is older than T. If the sum of the ages of R and T is 50 years, what is definitely the difference between R and Q's age? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Given that: 1. The difference of age b/w R and Q = The difference of age b/w Q and T. 2. Sum of age of R and T is 50 i.e. (R + T) = 50. Question: R - Q = ?. Explanation: R - Q = Q - T (R + T) = 2Q Now given that, (R + T) = 50 So, 50 = 2Q and therefore Q = 25. Question is (R - Q) = ? Here we know the value (age) of Q (25) , but we don't know the age of R. Therefore, (R-Q) cannot be determined.

question: The age of father 10 years ago was thrice the age of his son. Ten years hence, father's age will be twice that of his son. The ratio of their present ages is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Let the ages of father and son 10 years ago be 3x and x years respectively. (3x + 10) + 10 = 2[ (x + 10) + 10] 3x + 20 = 2x + 40 x = 20 Required ratio = (3x + 10) : (x + 10) = 70 : 30 = 7 : 3. ```

question: It was  on Jan 1, 2006. What was the day of the week Jan 1, 2010? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: On 31^ st December, 2005 it was Saturday. Number of odd days from the year 2006 to the year 2009 = (1 + 1 + 2 + 1) = 5 days. On 31^ st December 2009, it was Thursday. Thus, on 1^ st Jan, 2010 it is Friday.

question: What was the day of the week on 28th May, 2006? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex 28 May, 2006 = (2005 years + Period from 1.1.2006 to 28.5.2006) Odd days in 1600 years = 0 Odd days in 400 years = 0 5 years = (4 ordinary years + 1 leap year) = (4 × 1 + 1 × 2) = 6 odd days Jan. quad Feb. quad March quad April quad May (31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 28) = 148 days 148 days = (21 weeks + 1 day) = 1 odd day. Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 6 + 1) = 7 = 0 odd day. Given day is Sunday. ```

question: 3. What was the day of the week on 17th June, 1998? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 17^ th June, 1998 = (1997 years + Period from 1.1.1998 to 17.6.1998) Odd days in 1600 years = 0 Odd days in 300 years = (5 × 3) mod 1 = 1 97 years has 24 leap years + 73 ordinary years. Number of odd days in 97 years ( 24 x 2 + 73) = 121 = 2 odd days. Jan. Feb. March April May June (31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 17) = 168 days 168 days = 24 weeks = 0 odd day. Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 0) = 3. Given day is Wednesday.

question: 4. What will be the day of the week 15th August, 2010? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex 15^ th August, 2010 = (2009 years + Period 1.1.2010 to 15.8.2010) Odd days in 1600 years = 0 Odd days in 400 years = 0 9 years = (2 leap years + 7 ordinary years) = (2 × 2 + 7 × 1) = 11 odd days equiv 4 odd days. Jan.~ Feb.~ March~ April~ May~ June~ July~ Aug.~ (31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 15) = 227 days 227 days = (32 weeks + 3 days) equiv 3 odd days. Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 4 + 3) = 7 equiv 0 odd days. Given day is Sunday. ```

question: 5. Today is Monday. After 61 days, it will be: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Each day of the week is repeated after 7 days. So, after 63 days, it will be Monday. After 61 days, it will be Saturday. ```

question: If 6^ th March, 2005 is Monday, what was the day of the week on 6^ th March, 2004? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex 2004 年是一个闰年。因此，它有2个奇数天。 但是，2004年2月不包括在内，因为我们是从2004年3月计算到2005年3月。所以它只有1个奇数天。 2005年3月6日的日期比2004年3月6日的日期多1天。 已知2005年3月6日是星期一。 因此，2004年3月6日是星期日（比2005年3月6日早1天）。 ```

question: On what dates of April, 2001 did Wednesday fall? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: We shall find the day on 1^ st April, 2001. 1^ st April, 2001 = (2000 years + Period from 1.1.2001 to 1.4.2001) Odd days in 1600 years = 0 Odd days in 400 years = 0 Jan. Feb. March April (31 + 28 + 31 + 1) = 91 days Rarrow 0 odd days. Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 0) = 0 On 1^ st April, 2001 it was Sunday. In April, 2001 Wednesday falls on 4^ th, 11^ th, 18^ th and 25^ th.

question: How many days are there in x weeks x days? A.  B.  C.  D. 7
explanation: x weeks x days = (7 x + x) days = 8 x days.

question: The last day of a century cannot be A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex 100 years contain 5 odd days. Last day of 1^ st century is Friday. 200 years contain (5 × 2) equiv 3 odd days. Last day of 2^ nd century is Wednesday. 300 years contain (5 × 3) = 15 equiv 1 odd day. Last day of 3^ rd century is Monday. 400 years contain 0 odd day. Last day of 4^ th century is Sunday. This cycle is repeated. Last day of a century cannot be Tuesday or Thursday or Saturday. ```

question: On 8^ th Feb, 2005 it was . What was the day of the week on 8^ th Feb, 2004? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The year 2004 is a leap year. It has 2 odd days. The day on 8<sup>th</sup> Feb, 2004 is 2 days before the day on 8<sup>th</sup> Feb, 2005. Hence, this day is Sunday.

question: 11.quad The calendar for the year 2007 will be the same for the year: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Count the number of odd days from the year 2007 onwards to get the sum equal to 0 odd day. Year : 2007 2008 2009 2010 2011 2012 2013 2014 2015 2016 2017 Odd day : 1 2 1 1 1 2 1 1 1 2 1 Sum = 14 odd days equiv 0 odd days. Calendar for the year 2018 will be the same as for the year 2007.

question: Which of the following is not a leap year? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The century divisible by 400 is a leap year. The year 700 is not a leap year.

question: 13. On 8<sup>th</sup> Dec, 2007 Saturday falls. What day of the week was it on 8<sup>th</sup> Dec, 2006? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The year 2006 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day. So, the day on 8^th Dec, 2007 will be 1 day beyond the day on 8^th Dec, 2006. But, 8^th Dec, 2007 is Saturday. Therefore, 8^th Dec, 2006 is Friday.

question: 14. January 1, 2008 is Tuesday. What day of the week lies on Jan 1, 2009? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The year 2008 is a leap year. So, it has 2 odd days. 1^st day of the year 2008 is Tuesday (Given) So, 1^st day of the year 2009 is 2 days beyond Tuesday. Hence, it will be Thursday.

question: January 1, 2007 was . What day of the week lies on Jan. 1, 2008? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The year 2007 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day. 1^st day of the year 2007 was Monday. 1^st day of the year 2008 will be 1 day beyond Monday. Hence, it will be Tuesday.

question: An accurate clock shows 8 o'clock in the morning. Through how may degrees will the hour hand rotate when the clock shows 2 o'clock in the afternoon? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Angle t/ed by the hour hand in 6 hours = /36012 × 6 = 180°.

question: The reflex angle between the hands of a clock at 10.25 is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Angle t/ed by hour hand in 125 hrs = ( /36012 × 125 ) ^circ = 312 /12^circ. Angle t/ed by minute hand in 25 min = ( /36060 × 25 ) ^circ = 150^circ. Reflex angle = 360^circ - (312 /12 - 150 ) ^circ = 360^circ - 162 /12^circ = 197 /12^circ.

question: A clock is started at noon. By 10 minutes past 5, the hour hand has turned through: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Angle t/ed by hour hand in 12 hrs = 360°. Angle t/ed by hour hand in 5 hrs 10 min. i.e., 31 hrs = ( /36012 × 31 ) ° = 155°.

question: 4. A watch which gains 5 seconds in 3 minutes was set r at 7 a.m. In the afternoon of the same day, when the watch indicated quarter past 4 o'clock, the true time is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Time from 7 a.m. to 4.15 p.m. = 9 hrs 15 min. = 9 /14 hrs. 3 min. 5 sec. of this clock = 3 min. of the correct clock. 37 hrs of this clock = /1720 × 37 hrs of the correct clock. 37 hrs of this clock = /1720 × 20 × 37 × /7204 × 37 hrs of the correct clock. = 9 hrs of the correct clock. The correct time is 9 hrs after 7 a.m. iti.e., 4 p.m. ```

question: How much does a watch lose per day, if its hands coincide every 64 minutes? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 60 min. spaces are covered in 60 min. 60 min. spaces are covered in ( /6055 × 60 ) min. 60 min. spaces are covered in 65 /511 min. Loss in 64 min. = (65 /511 - 64 ) min. Loss in 64 min. = /1611 min. Loss in 24 hrs = ( /1611 × 1 × 24 × 60 ) min. Loss in 24 hrs = 32 /811 min.

question: 6. At what time between 7 and 8 o'clock will the hands of a clock be in the same stra line but, not together? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: When the hands of the clock are in the same stra line but not together, they are 30 minute spaces apart. At 7 o'clock, they are 25 min. spaces apart. Minute hand will have to gain only 5 min. spaces. 55 min. spaces are gained in 60 min. 5 min. spaces are gained in ( /6055 × 5 ) min = 5 min. Required time = 5 min. past 7.

question: 7. At what time between 5.30 and 6 will the hands of a clock be at r angles? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: At 5 o'clock, the hands are 25 min. spaces apart. To be at r angles and that too between 5.30 and 6, the minute hand has to gain (25 + 15) = 40 min. spaces. 55 min. spaces are gained in 60 min. 40 min. spaces are gained in ( /6055 × 40 ) min = 43 /711 min. Required time = 43 /711 min. past 5.

question: The angle between the minute hand and the hour hand of a clock when the time is 4.20, is: A.  B. 1 C.  D. 2
explanation: Angle t/ed by hour hand in 13 hrs = ( /36012 × 13 ) ^circ = 130^circ. Angle t/ed by min. hand in 20 min. = ( /36060 × 20 ) ^circ = 120^circ. Required angle = (130 - 120) ^circ = 10^circ.

question: At what angle the hands of a clock are inclined at 15 minutes past 5? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Angle t/ed by hour hand in 21 hrs = ( /36012 × 21 ) ^circ = 157 /12^circ Angle t/ed by min. hand in 15 min. = ( /36060 × 15 ) ^circ = 90^circ Required angle = ( 157 /12^circ - 90^circ ) = 67 /12^circ

question: 10. At 3:40, the hour hand and the minute hand of a clock form an angle of: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 13. How many t× in a day, are the hands of a clock in stra line but opposite in direction? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 14. At what time between 4 and 5 o'clock will the hands of a watch point in opposite directions? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: At 4 o'clock, the hands of the watch are 20 min. spaces apart. To be in opposite directions, they must be 30 min. spaces apart. Minute hand will have to gain 50 min. spaces. 55 min. spaces are gained in 60 min. 50 min. spaces are gained in ( /6055 × 50 ) min. or 54 /611 min. Required time = 54 /611 min. past 4.

question: 15. At what time between 9 and 10 o'clock will the hands of a watch be together? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: To be together between 9 and 10 o'clock, the minute hand has to gain 45 min. spaces. 55 min. spaces gained in 60 min. 45 min. spaces are gained in ( /6055 × 45 ) min or 49 /111 min. The hands are together at 49 /111 min. past 9.

question: At what time, in minutes, between 3 o'clock and 4 o'clock, both the needles will coincide each other? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: At 3 o'clock, the minute hand is 15 min. spaces apart from the hour hand. To be coincident, it must gain 15 min. spaces. 55 min. are gained in 60 min. 15 min. are gained in ( /6055 × 15 ) min = 16 /411 min. The hands are coincident at 16 /411 min. past 3.

question: 17. How many t× do the hands of a clock coincide in a day? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 18. How many t× in a day, the hands of a clock are stra? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 19. A watch which gains uniformly is 2 minutes low at noon on Monday and is 4 min. 48 sec fast at 2 p.m. on the following Monday. When was it correct? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Time from 12 p.m. on Monday to 2 p.m. on the following Monday = 7 days 2 hours = 170 hours. The watch gains ( 2 + 4 ) min. or 34 min. in 170 hrs. Now, /345 min. are gained in 170 hrs. 2 min. are gained in ( 170 × /534 × 2 ) hrs = 50 hrs. Watch is correct 2 days 2 hrs. after 12 p.m. on Monday i.e., it will be correct at 2 p.m. on Wednesday. ```

question: 6. The captain of a cricket team of 11 members is 26 years old and the wicket keeper is 3 years older. If the ages of these two are excluded, the average age of the remaining players is one year less than the average age of the whole team. What is the average age of the team? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the average age of the whole team by x years. 11x - (26 + 29) = 9 (x -1) 11x - 9x = 46 2x = 46 x = 23 So, average age of the team is 23 years.

question: 7. The average monthly income of P and Q is Rs. 5050. The average monthly income of Q and R is Rs. 6250 and the average monthly income of P and R is Rs. 5200. The monthly income of P is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let P, Q and R represent their respective monthly incomes. Then, we have: P + Q = (5050 x 2) = 10100 .... (i) Q + R = (6250 x 2) = 12500 .... (ii) P + R = (5200 x 2) = 10400 .... (iii) Adding (i) , (ii) and (iii) , we get: 2 (P + Q + R) = 33000 or P + Q + R = 16500 .... (iv) Subt/ting (ii) from (iv) , we get P = 4000. P's monthly income = Rs. 4000.

question: The average age of husband, wife and their child 3 years ago was 27 years and that of wife and the child 5 years ago was 20 years. The present age of the husband is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Sum of the present ages of husband, wife and child = (27 × 3 + 3 × 3) years = 90 years. Sum of the present ages of wife and child = (20 × 2 + 5 × 2) years = 50 years. Husband's present age = (90 - 50) years = 40 years.

question: A car owner buys petrol at Rs.7.50,  and .50 per litre for three successive years. What approximately is the average cost per litre of petrol if he spends Rs. 4000 each year? A.  B.  C. .50 D.

question: 11. The average we of A, B and C is 45 kg. If the average we of A and B be 40 kg and that of B and C be 43 kg, then the we of B is: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: The ratio between the length and the breadth of a rectangular park is 3 : 2. If a man cycling along the boundary of the park at the speed of 12 km/hr completes one round in 8 minutes, then the area of the park (in sq. m) is: A.  B. 0 C.  D. 0

question: A rectangular park 60 m long and 40 m wide has two concrete crossroads running in the middle of the park and rest of the park has been used as a lawn. If the area of the lawn is 2109 sq. m, then what is the width of the road? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 13. The length of a rectangular plot is 20 metres more than its breadth. If the cost of fencing the plot @ 26. per metre is Rs. 5300, what is the length of the plot in metres? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.

question: Which one of the following is not a prime number? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 91 is divisible by 7. So, it is not a prime number.

question: It is being given that (2^32 + 1) is completely divisible by a whole number. Which of the following numbers is completely divisible by this number? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 2^32 = x. (2^32 + 1) = (x + 1) . Let (x + 1) be completely divisible by the natural number N. Then, 2^96 + 1 = [ (2^32) ^3 + 1] = (x^3 + 1) = (x + 1) (x^2 - x + 1) , which is completely divisible by N, since (x + 1) is divisible by N.

question: What least number must be added to 1056, so that the sum is completely divisible by 23? A. 2 B. 3 C.  D.  E.
explanation: Required number = (23 - 21) = 2

question: How many of the following numbers are divisible by 132 ? 264, 396, 462, 792, 968, 2178, 5184, 6336 A. 4 B. 5 C. 6 D. 7
explanation: 132 = 4 × 3 × 11 So, if the number divisible by all the three numbers 4, 3, and 11, then the number is divisible by 132 also. 264 div 11,3,4 396 div 11,3,4 462 div 11,3 quad ( X) 792 div 11,3,4 968 div 11,4 quad ( X) 2178 div 11,3 quad ( X) 5184 div 3,4 quad ( X) 6336 div 11,3,4 Therefore, the following numbers are divisible by 132: 264, 396, 792, and 6336. Required number of numbers = 4.

question: What is the unit digit in (6374) ^1793 × (625) ^317 × (341) ^491? A. 0 B. 2 C. 3 D. 5

question: The sum of first five prime numbers is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Required sum = (2 + 3 + 5 + 7 + 11) = 28. Note: 1 is not a prime number. Definition: A prime number (or a prime) is a natural number that has exactly two distinct natural number divisors: 1 and itself.

question: The difference of two numbers is 1365. On dividing the larger number by the smaller, we get 6 as quotient and the 15 as remainder. What is the smaller number? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: x + 1365 = 6x + 15 5x = 1350 x = 270 Smaller number = 270

question: If the number 517ifmmodestelse* i324 is completely divisible by 3, then the smallest whole number in the place of ifmmodestelse* i will be: A. 0 B. 1 C. 2 D.

question: The smallest 3 digit prime number is:  A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex 100 is the smallest 3-digit number and is divisible by 2. 100 is not a prime number. 101 < 11 and 101 is not divisible by any of the prime numbers 2, 3, 5, 7, 11. 101 is a prime number. Hence, 101 is the smallest 3-digit prime number. ```

question: 26. The difference between a positive proper f/tion and its reciprocal is 0.45. The f/tion is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the required f/tion be x. Then  eginarraycccc 1 & - & x & = & & x & 20 endarray  eginarraycccc & & 1 - x^2 & = & & x & 20 endarray Therefore, 20 - 20x^2 = 9x Therefore, 20x^2 + 9x - 20 = 0 Therefore, 20x^2 + 25x - 16x - 20 = 0 Therefore, 5x (4x + 5) - 4 (4x + 5) = 0 Therefore, (4x + 5) (5x - 4) = 0 Thus, x = /45

question: On dividing a number by 56, we get 29 as remainder. On dividing the same number by 8, what will be the remainder? A. 4 B. 5 C. 6 D. 7
explanation: Formula: (Divisor*Quotient) + Remainder = Dividend. Soln: (56*Q) +29 = D quad ------- (1) D%8 = R quad ------------- (2) From equation (2) , ( (56*Q) +29) %8 = R. Assume Q = 1. Then, (56+29) %8 = R. 85%8 = R 5 = R.

question: 28. If n is a natural number, then (6n^2 + 6n) is always divisible by: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 6n^2 + 6n = 6n (n + 1) , which is always divisible by 6 and 12 both, since n (n + 1) is always even.

question: What will be remainder when (^ + ) is divided by 68? A. 1 B.  C.  D.
explanation: (x^n + 1 ) will be divisible by (x + 1 ) only when n is odd. (67^67 + 1 ) will be divisible by (67 + 1 ) (67^67 + 1 ) + 66, when divided by 68 will give 66 as remainder.

question: On dividing a number by 5, we get 3 as remainder. What will the remainder when the square of the this number is divided by 5? A. 0 B. 1 C. 2 D. 4
explanation: Let the number be x and on dividing x by 5, we get k as quotient and 3 as remainder. Then, x = 5k + 3 Therefore, x^2 = (5k + 3) ^2 = (25k^2 + 30k + 9) = 5 (5k^2 + 6k + 1) + 4 On dividing x^2 by 5, we get 4 as remainder.

question: 32. How many 3-digit numbers are completely divisible by 6? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 3-digit number divisible by 6 are: 102, 108, 114,... , 996 This is an A.P. in which a = 102, d = 6 and l = 996 Let the number of terms be n. Then t_n = 996. a + (n - 1) d = 996 102 + (n - 1) × 6 = 996 6 × (n - 1) = 894 (n - 1) = 149 n = 150 Number of terms = 150.

question: 33. How many natural numbers are there between 23 and 100 which are exactly divisible by 6? A. 8 B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Required numbers are 24, 30, 36, 42, ..., 96 This is an A.P. in which a = 24, d = 6 and l = 96 Let the number of terms in it be n. t_n = 96 Rarrow a + (n - 1) d = 96 Rarrow 24 + (n - 1) × 6 = 96 Rarrow (n - 1) × 6 = 72 Rarrow (n - 1) = 12 Rarrow n = 13 Required number of numbers = 13.

question: 34.~ How many of the following numbers are divisible by 3 but not by 9 ? 2133, 2343, 3474, 4131, 5286, 5340, 6336, 7347, 8115, 9276 A. 5 B. 6 C. 7 D.

question: 36. How many 3 digit numbers are divisible by 6 in all? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Required numbers are 102, 108, 114, ..., 996 This is an A.P. in which a = 102, d = 6, and l = 996 Let the number of terms be n. a + (n - 1) d = 996 102 + (n - 1) × 6 = 996 6 × (n - 1) = 894 (n - 1) = 149 n = 150.

question: 37. A 3-digit number 4 ita 3 is added to another 3-digit number 984 to give a 4-digit number 13 itb 7, which is divisible by . Then, ( ita + itb) = ? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: 4 a 3 | 9 8 4 Rarrow a + 8 = b Rarrow b - a = 8 13 b 7 | Also, 13 b7 is divisible by 11 Rarrow (7 + 3) - (b + 1) = (9 - b) Rarrow (9 - b) = 0 Rarrow b = 9 Rarrow (b = 9 and a = 1) Rarrow (a + b) = 10.

question: The smallest prime number is: A. 1 B. 2 C. 3 D. 4
explanation: The smallest prime number is 2.

question: On dividing a number by 357, we get 39 as remainder. On dividing the same number 17, what will be the remainder? A. 0 B. 3 C. 5 D.
explanation: Let x be the number and y be the quotient. Then, x = 357 cdot y + 39 = (17 cdot 21 cdot y) + (17 cdot 2) + 5 = 17 cdot (21y + 2) + 5) Required remainder = 5.

question: 42. If the product 4864 x 9 P 2 is divisible by 12, then the value of P is: A. 2 B. 5 C. 6 D. 8 E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, 4864 is divisible by 4. So, 9P2 must be divisible by 3. So, (9 + P + 2) must be divisible by 3. P = 1.

question: 43.~ Which one of the following is the common factor of~  ~ and~ (47^47 + 43^47) ~? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: When n is odd, (x^n + a^n) is always divisible by (x + a) . Each one of (47^43 + 43^43) and (47^47 + 43^47) is divisible by (47 + 43) .

question: 45. A number when divided by 296 leaves 75 as remainder. When the same number is divided by 37, the remainder will be: A. 1 B. 2 C. 8 D.
explanation: Let x = 296q + 75 = (37 × 8q + 37 × 2) + 1 = 37 (8q + 2) + 1 Thus, when the number is divided by 37, the remainder is 1.

question: 46.~ In dividing a number by 585, a student employed the method of short division. He divided the number successively by 5, 9 and 13 (factors 585) and got the remainders 4, 8, 12 respectively. If he had divided the number by 585, the remainder would have been A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: eginarrayc|c 5 & x & z = 13x1 + 12 = 25 hline 9 & y - 4 & y = 9x25 + 8 = 233 hline 13 & z - 8 & x = 5x233 + 4 = 1169 hline & 1 - 12 endarray  eginarrayr|r 585 & 1169 & 585 hline & 584 endarray Therefore, on dividing the number by 585, remainder = 584.

51. 476 ** 0 is divisible by both 3 and 11. The non-zero digits in the hundred's and ten's places are respectively: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Let the given number be 476 xy 0. Then (4 + 7 + 6 + x + y + 0) = (17 + x + y ) must be divisible by 3. And, (0 + x + 7) - (y + 6 + 4) = (x - y -3) must be either 0 or 11. x - y - 3 = 0 quad Rarrow quad y = x - 3 (17 + x + y ) = (17 + x + x - 3) = (2x + 14) Rarrow x = 2 or x = 8. Rarrow x = 8 and y = 5. ```

question: If the number 97215 * 6 is completely divisible by 11, then the smallest whole number in place of * will be: A. 3 B. 2 C. 1 D. 5 E.

question: 54. If the number 5 * 2 is divisible by 6, then * = ? A. 2 B. 3 C. 6 D. 7
explanation: 6 = 3 x 2. Clearly, 5 * 2 is divisible by 2. Replace * by x . Then, (5 + x + 2) must be divisible by 3. So, x = 2.

question: 55.~ Which of the following numbers will completely divide~ (49^15 - 1) ~? A. 8 B.  C.  D.
explanation: (x^n - 1 ) will be divisible by (x + 1 ) only when n is even. (49^15 - 1) = (7^2) ^15 - 1 = (7^30 - 1) , which is divisible by (7 + 1) , i.e., 8.

question: /1n (1 - /12 ) + /1n (1 - /13 ) + /1n (1 - /14 ) + ldots up to n terms = ? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 58. On dividing 2272 as well as 875 by 3-digit number N, we get the same remainder. The sum of the digits of N is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Clearly, (2272 - 875) = 1397, is exactly divisible by N. Now, 1397 = 11 x 127 The required 3-digit number is 127, the sum of whose digits is 10.

question: 59. A boy multiplied 987 by a certain number and obtained 559981 as his answer. If in the answer both 9 are wrong and the other digits are correct, then the correct answer would be: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex 987 = 3 × 7 × 47 So, the required number must be divisible by each one of 3, 7, 47 553681 (Sum of digits = 28, not divisible by 3) 555181 (Sum of digits = 25, not divisible by 3) 555681 is divisible by 3, 7, 47. ```

question: 61. When a number is divided by 13, the remainder is 11. When the same number is divided by 17, then remainder is 9. What is the number? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: x = 13p + 11 quad and quad x = 17q + 9 13p + 11 = 17q + 9 17q - 13p = 2 q = /2 + 13p17 The least value of (p) for which (q) is a whole number is (p = 26) x = (13 × 26 + 11) = (338 + 11) = 349

76. A number when divided by 6 leaves a remainder 3. When the square of the number is divided by 6, the remainder is: A. 0 B. 1 C. 2 D. 3
explanation: Let x = 6q + 3. Then, x^2 = (6q + 3) ^2 = 36q^2 + 36q + 9 = 6 (6q^2 + 6q + 1) + 3 Thus, when x^2 is divided by 6, then remainder = 3.

question: Let the two numbers be x and y. x + y = 12 xy = 35 The sum of the reciprocals is /1x + /1y = /x + yxy = /1235 Therefore, the answer is /1235. A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be a and b. Then, a + b = 12 and ab = 35. Sum of reciprocals of given numbers = /1a + /1b = /a+bab = /1235.

question: 78. What will be remainder when ^200 is divided by 18? A.  B.  C. 1 D. 2
explanation: When n is even, (x^n - a^n) is completely divisible by (x + a) . (17^200 - 1^200) is completely divisible by (17 + 1) , i.e., 18. implies (17^200 - 1) is completely divisible by 18. implies On dividing 17^200 by 18, we get 1 as remainder.

question: 81. The difference of the squares of two consecutive even integers is divisible by which of the following integers? A. 3 B. 4 C. 6 D. 7
explanation: Let the two consecutive even integers be 2n and (2n + 2) . Then, (2n + 2) ^2 = (2n + 2 + 2n) (2n + 2 - 2n) = 2 (4n + 2) = 4 (2n + 1) , which is divisible by 4.

question: Which one of the following is a prime number? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: 551 > 22 All prime numbers less than 24 are : 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19, 23. 119 is divisible by 7; 187 is divisible by 11; 247 is divisible by 13 and 551 is divisible by 19. So, none of the given numbers is prime.

question: The sum of all even natural numbers between 1 and 31 is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Required sum = (2 + 4 + 6 + ldots + 30) This is an A.P. in which a = 2, d = (4 - 2) = 2, and l = 30. Let the number of terms be n. t_n = 30 Rarrow a + (n - 1) d = 30 Rarrow 2 + (n - 1) cdot 2 = 30 Rarrow n - 1 = 14 Rarrow n = 15 Sum S_n = /n (a + l) 2 = /15 cdot (2 + 30) 2 = 240.

question: The difference between the place value and the face value of 6 in the numeral 856 is A.  B. 6 C.  D.

question: If a and b are odd numbers, then which of the following is even ? A.  B.  + 1 C.  D.  + 2 E.
explanation: The sum of two odd numbers is even. So, a + b is even.

question: 86. Which one of the following numbers is completely divisible by 99? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: 99 = 11 x 9, where 11 and 9 are co-prime. By hit and trial, we find that 114345 is divisible by 11 as well as 9. So, it is divisible by 99.

What is the unit digit in (7^95 - 3^58) ? A. 0 B. 4 C. 6 D. 7
explanation: Unit digit in 7^95 = Unit digit in [ (7^4) ^23 × 7^3] = Unit digit in [ (2401) ^23 × 343] = Unit digit in (1^23 × 343) = Unit digit in (343) = 3 Unit digit in 3^58 = Unit digit in [ (3^4) ^14 × 3^2] = Unit digit in [ (81) ^14 × 3^2] = Unit digit in [ (1) ^14 × 3^2] = Unit digit in (1 × 9) = Unit digit in (9) = 9 Unit digit in (7^95 - 3^58) = Unit digit in (343 - 9) = Unit digit in (334) = 4. So, Option B is the answer.

question: 97.~ If x and y are positive integers such that (3x + 7y) is a multiple of 11, then which of the following will be divisible by 11? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: (3x + 7y) = (3 cdot 5 + 7 cdot 1) = 22, which is divisible by 11. (4x + 6y) = (4 cdot 5 + 6 cdot 1) = 26, which is not divisible by 11; (x + y + 4) = (5 + 1 + 4) = 10, which is not divisible by 11; (9x + 4y) = (9 cdot 5 + 4 cdot 1) = 49, which is not divisible by 11; (4x - 9y) = (4 cdot 5 - 9 cdot 1) = 11, which is divisible by 11.

question: The sum of even numbers between 1 and 31 is: A. 6 B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let S_n = (2 + 4 + 6 + ... + 30) . This is an A.P. in which a = 2, d = 2, and l = 30 Let the number of terms be n. Then, a + (n - 1) d = 30 2 + (n - 1) × 2 = 30 n = 15. Thus, S_n = /n2 (a + l) = /152 × (2 + 30) = (15 × 16) = 240.

question: 106. If x and y are the two digits of the number 653x y such that this number is divisible by 80, then x + y = ? A.  B. 4 C.  D. 8 E.
explanation: 80 = 2 x 5 x 8 Since 653 itxy is divisible by 2 and 5 both, so ity = 0. Now, 653 itx is divisible by 8, so 13 itx should be divisible by 8. This happens when itx = 6. Therefore, itx + ity = (6 + 0) = 6.

question: 107. The difference of the squares of two consecutive odd integers is divisible by which of the following integers? A. 3 B. 6 C. 7 D. 8
explanation: Let the two consecutive odd integers be (2n + 1) and (2n + 3) . Then, (2n + 3) ^2 - (2n + 1) ^2 = (2n + 3 + 2n + 1) (2n + 3 - 2n - 1) = (4n + 4) × 2 = 8 (n + 1) , which is divisible by 8.

110. A number was divided successively in order by 4, 5 and 6. The remainders were respectively 2, 3 and 4. The number is: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: The sum of first 45 natural numbers is: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 118. The sum of all two digit numbers divisible by 5 is: A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Required numbers are 10, 15, 20, 25, ..., 95 This is an A.P. in which a = 10, d = 5 and l = 95. t_n = 95 implies a + (n - 1) d = 95 implies 10 + (n - 1) × 5 = 95 implies (n - 1) × 5 = 85 implies (n - 1) = 17 implies n = 18 Required Sum = /n2 (a + l) = /182 × (10 + 95) = 9 × 105 = 945.

question: 120. On dividing a number by 68, we get 269 as quotient and 0 as remainder. On dividing the same number by 67, what will the remainder ? A. 0 B. 1 C. 2 D. 3
121. What is the unit digit in the product (3^65 × 6^59 × 7^71 ) ? A. 1 B. 2 C. 4 D. 6
explanation: Unit digit in 3^4 = 1 Unit digit in (3^4) ^16 = 1 Unit digit in 3^65 = Unit digit in [ (3^4) ^16 × 3] = (1 × 3) = 3 Unit digit in 6^59 = 6 Unit digit in 7^4 = 1 Unit digit in (7^4) ^17 = 1 Unit digit in 7^71 = Unit digit in [ (7^4) ^17 × 7^3] = (1 × 3) = 3 Required digit = Unit digit in (3 × 6 × 3) = 4

question: If the number 653 itxy is divisible by 90, then ( itx + ity) = ? A. 2 B. 3 C. 4 D. 6
explanation: 90 = 10 x 9 Clearly, 653 itxy is divisible by 10, so ity = 0 Now, 653 itx0 is divisible by 9. So, (6 + 5 + 3 + itx + 0) = (14 + itx ) is divisible by 9. So, itx = 4. Hence, ( itx + ity ) = (4 + 0) = 4.

question: 128. What is the unit digit in 7^105 ? A. 1 B. 5 C. 7 D. 9

question: 136. Which one of the following can't be the square of natural number? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The square of a natural number never ends in 2. 143642 is not the square of a natural number.

question: If one-third of one-fourth of a number is 15, then three-tenth of that number is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the number be x. Then, /13 of /14 of x = 15 implies x = 15 × 3 × 4 = 180. So, required number = ( /310 × 180 ) = 54.

question: 2 cdot third integer + 3 = 3 cdot first integer A. 9 B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the three integers be x, x + 2 and x + 4. Then, 3x = 2 (x + 4) + 3 implies x = 11. Third integer = x + 4 = 15.

question: The difference between a two-digit number and the number obtained by interchanging the positions of its digits is 36. What is the difference between the two digits of that number? A. 3 B. 4 C. 9 D.  E.
explanation: Let the ten's digit be x and unit's digit be y. Then, (10x + y) - (10y + x) = 36 9 (x - y) = 36 x - y = 4.

question: 4. The difference between a two-digit number and the number obtained by interchanging the digits is 36. What is the difference between the sum and the difference of the digits of the number if the ratio between the digits of the number is 1 : 2 ? A. 4 B. 8 C.  D.
explanation: Since the number is greater than the number obtained on reversing the digits, so the ten's digit is greater than the unit's digit. Let ten's and unit's digits be 2x and x respectively. Then, (10 × 2x + x) - (10x + 2x) = 36 implies 9x = 36 implies x = 4 Required difference = (2x + x) - (2x - x) = 2x = 8

question: A two-digit number is such that the product of the digits is 8. When  is added to the number, then the digits are reversed. The number is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the ten's and unit digit be x and respectively. Then, (10x + 8 ) + 18 = 10 × 8 + x Rarrow 10x^2 + 8 + 18x = 80 + x^2 Rarrow 9x^2 + 18x - 72 = 0 Rarrow x^2 + 2x - 8 = 0 Rarrow (x + 4) (x - 2) = 0 Rarrow x = 2.

question: 6. The sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 15 and the difference between the digits is 3. What is the two-digit number? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Let the ten's digit be x and unit's digit be y. Then, x + y = 15 and x - y = 3 or y - x = 3. Solving x + y = 15 and x - y = 3, we get: x = 9, y = 6. Solving x + y = 15 and y - x = 3, we get: x = 6, y = 9. So, the number is either 96 or 69. Hence, the number cannot be determined.

question: The sum of the squares of three numbers is 138, while the sum of their products taken two at a time is 131. Their sum is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be a, b and c. a^2 + b^2 + c^2 = 138 and (ab + bc + ca) = 131. (a + b + c) ^2 = a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + 2 (ab + bc + ca) = 138 + 2 × 131 = 400. Therefore, (a + b + c) = sqrt400 = 20. Video Explanation: hrefhttps://youtu.be/qmJ-0X8j_xQ https://youtu.be/qmJ-0X8j_xQ

question: A number consists of two digits. If the digits interchange places and the new number is added to the original number, then the resulting number will be divisible by: A. 3 B. 5 C. 9 D.
explanation: Let the ten's digit be x and unit's digit be y. Then, number = 10x + y. Number obtained by interchanging the digits = 10y + x. (10x + y) + (10y + x) = 11 (x + y) , which is divisible by 11.

question: In a two-digit number, if it is known that its unit's digit exceeds its ten's digit by 2 and that the product of the given number and the sum of its digits is equal to 144, then the number is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the ten's digit be x. Then, unit's digit = x + 2. Number = 10x + (x + 2) = 11x + 2. Sum of digits = x + (x + 2) = 2x + 2. (11x + 2) (2x + 2) = 144 22x^2 + 26x - 140 = 0 11x^2 + 13x - 70 = 0 (x - 2) (11x + 35) = 0 x = 2. Hence, required number = 11x + 2 = 24.

question: Find a positive number which when increased by  is equal to 60 t× the reciprocal of the number. A. 3 B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the number be x. Then, x + 17 = 60 div x Hence, x^2 + 17x - 60 = 0 Thus, (x + 20) (x - 3) = 0 Therefore, x = 3.

question: 11. The product of two numbers is 9375 and the quotient, when the larger one is divided by the smaller, is 15. The sum of the numbers is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be x and y. Then, xy = 9375 and x = 15. xy = 9375 (x/y) = 15 y^2 = 625. y = 25. x = 15y = (15 × 25) = 375. Sum of the numbers = x + y = 375 + 25 = 400.

question: The product of two numbers is 1 and the sum of their squares is 289. The sum of the number is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be x and y. x y = 120 and x^2 + y^2 = 289. (x + y) ^2 = x^2 + y^2 + 2xy = 289 + (2 × 120) = 529 x + y = sqrt529 = 23.

question: A number consists of 3 digits whose sum is 10. The middle digit is equal to the sum of the other two and the number will be increased by 99 if its digits are reversed. The number is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the middle digit be x. Then, 2x = 10 or x = 5. So, the number is either 253 or 352. Since the number increases on reversing the digits, so the hundred's digit is smaller than the unit's digit. Hence, required number = 253.

question: 14. The sum of two numbers is 25 and their difference is 13. Find their product. A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be x and y. Then, x + y = 25 and x - y = 13. 4xy = (x + y) ^2 - (x - y) ^2 = (25) ^2 - (13) ^2 = (625 - 169) = 456 xy = 114.

question: What is the sum of two consecutive even numbers, the difference of whose squares is 84? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be x and x + 2. (x + 2) ^2 - x^2 = 84 implies 4x + 4 = 84 implies 4x = 80 implies x = 20. The required sum = x + (x + 2) = 2x + 2 = 42.

question: Find the greatest number that will divide 43, 91 and 183 so as to leave the same remainder in each case. A. 4 B. 7 C. 9 D.
explanation: Required number = H.C.F. of (91 - 43) , (183 - 91) and (183 - 43) quad = H.C.F. of 48, 92 and 140 = 4.

question: 2. The H.C.F. of two numbers is 23 and the other two factors of their L.C.M. are 13 and 14. The larger of the two numbers is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Clearly, the numbers are (23 x 13) and (23 x 14) . Larger number = (23 x 14) = 322.

question: Six bells commence tolling together and toll at intervals of 2, 4, 6, 8  and 12 seconds respectively. In 30 minutes, how many t× do they toll together? A. 4 B.  C.  D.
explanation: L.C.M. of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 is 120. So, the bells will toll together after every 120 seconds (2 minutes) . In 30 minutes, they will toll together 30 div 2 + 1 = 16 t×.

The greatest number of four digits which is divisible by 15, 25, 40 and 75 is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Greatest number of 4-digits is 9999. L.C.M. of 15, 25, 40 and 75 is 600. On dividing 9999 by 600, the remainder is 399. Required number (9999 - 399) = 9600.

question: The product of two numbers is 4. If the H.C.F. of these numbers is 37, then the greater number is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be 37a and 37b. 37a × 37b = 4107 Then, ab = 3. Now, co-pr× with product 3 are (1, 3) . So, the required numbers are (37 x 1, 37 x 3) i.e., (37, 111) . Greater number = 111.

question: Three number are in the ratio of 3 : 4 : 5 and their L.C.M. is 20. Their H.C.F. is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the numbers be 3x, 4x and 5x. Then, their L.C.M. = 60x. So, 60x = 2400 or x = 40. The numbers are (3 × 40) , (4 × 40) and (5 × 40) . Hence, required H.C.F. = 40.

question: The G.C.D. of 1.08, 0.36 and  is: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 9. The product of two numbers is 2028 and their H.C.F. is 13. The number of such pairs is: A. 1 B. 2 C. 3 D. 4
explanation: Let the numbers 13a and 13b. Then, 13a × 13b = 2028 implies ab = 12. Now, the co-pr× with product 12 are (1, 12) and (3, 4) . So, the required numbers are (13 x 1, 13 x 12) and (13 x 3, 13 x 4) . Clearly, there are 2 such pairs.

question: The least multiple of 7, which leaves a remainder of 4, when divided by 6, 9, 15 and 18 is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: L.C.M. of 6, 9, 15 and 18 is 90. Let required number be 90k + 4, which is multiple of 7. Least value of k for which (90k + 4) is divisible by 7 is k = 4. Required number = (90 x 4) + 4 = 364.

The least number which should be added to 2497 so that the sum is exactly divisible by 5, 6, 4 and 3 is: A. 3 B.  C.  D.
explanation: L.C.M. of 5, 6, 4 and 3 = 60. On dividing 2497 by 60, the remainder is 37. Number to be added = (60 - 37) = 23.

14. The least number which when divided by 5, 6, 7, and 8 leaves a remainder 3, but when divided by 9 leaves no remainder, is: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 15. A, B and C start at the same time in the same direction to run around a circular stadium. A completes a round in 252 seconds, B in 308 seconds and c in 198 seconds, all starting at the same point. After what time will they again at the starting point? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 14. The price of commodity X increases by 40 paise every year, while the price of commodity Y increases by 15 paise every year. If in 2001, the price of commodity X was Rs. 4.20 and that of Y was Rs. 6.30, in which year commodity X will cost 40 paise more than the commodity Y ? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Suppose commodity X will cost 40 paise more than Y after z years. Then, (4.20 + 0.40z) - (6.30 + 0.15z) = 0.40 Therefore, 0.25z = 0.40 + 2.10 Thus, z = /2.500.25 = 10 Hence, X will cost 40 paise more than Y 10 years after 2001 i.e., 2011.

A man has Rs.480 in the denominations of one-rupee notes, five-rupee notes and ten-rupee notes. The number of notes of each denomination is equal. What is the total number of notes that he has? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let number of notes of each denomination be x. x + 5x + 10x = 480 16x = 480 x = 30 Hence, total number of notes = 3x = 90.

question: There are two examinations rooms A and B. If 10 students are sent from A to B, then the number of students in each room is the same. If  candidates are sent from B to A, then the number of students in A is double the number of students in B. The number of students in room A is: A.  B.  C.  D. 0
explanation: Let the number of students in rooms A and B be x and y respectively. Then, x - 10 = y + 10 quad Rarrow quad x - y = 20 quad .... (i) and x + 20 = 2 (y - 20) quad Rarrow quad x - 2y = -60 quad .... (ii) Solving (i) and (ii) we get: x = 100, y = 80. The required answer A = 100.

question: The price of 10 chairs is equal to that of 4 tables. The price of 15 chairs and 2 tables together is Rs. 4000. The total price of 12 chairs and 3 tables is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the cost of a chair and that of a table be Rs. x and Rs. y respectively. 10x = 4y quad or quad y = /52x. 15x + 2y = 4000 Rarrow 15x + 2 ( /52x ) = 4000 Rarrow 20x = 4000 Rarrow x = 200. So, y = ( /52 ) × 200 = 500. Hence, the cost of 12 chairs and 3 tables = 12x + 3y quad = Rs. (2400 + 1500) quad = Rs. 3900.

The price of 2 sarees and 4 shirts is Rs. 1600. With the same money one can buy 1 saree and 6 shirts. If one wants to buy 12 shirts, how much shall he have to pay ? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Let the price of a saree and a shirt be Rs. x and Rs. y respectively. Then, 2x + 4y = 1600 .... (i) and x + 6y = 1600 .... (ii) Divide equation (i) by 2, we get the below equation. = > x + 2y = 800. --- (iii) Now subt/t (iii) from (ii) x + 6y = 1600 (-) x + 2y = 800 ---------------- 4y = 800 ---------------- Therefore, y = 200. Now apply value of y in (iii) = > x + 2 × 200 = 800 = > x + 400 = 800 Therefore x = 400 Solving (i) and (ii) we get x = 400, y = 200. Cost of 12 shirts = Rs. (12 x 200) = Rs. 2400.

question: A sum of Rs. 1360 has been divided among A, B and C such that A gets /12 × /23 of what B gets and B gets /11 × /14 of what C gets. B's share is: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 11. In a regular week, there are 5 working days and for each day, the working hours are 8. A man gets Rs. 2.40 per hour for regular work and Rs. 3.20 per hours for overtime. If he earns Rs. 432 in 4 weeks, then how many hours does he work for? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Suppose the man works overtime for x hours. Now, working hours in 4 weeks = (5 x 8 x 4) = 160. 160 x 2.40 + x x 3.20 = 432 3.20 x = 432 - 384 = 48 x = 15. Hence, total hours of work = (160 + 15) = 175.

question: 9. Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 and 30 minutes respectively. If both the pipes are used together, then how long will it take to fill the tank? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Part filled by A in 1 min = /120 Part filled by B in 1 min = /130 Part filled by (A + B) in 1 min = ( /120 + /130 ) = /112 Both pipes can fill the tank in 12 minutes.

question: 10. Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 15 minutes and 20 minutes respectively. Both the pipes are opened together but after 4 minutes, pipe A is turned off. What is the total time required to fill the tank? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 12. A large tanker can be filled by two pipes A and B in 60 minutes and 40 minutes respectively. How many minutes will it take to fill the tanker from empty state if B is used for half the time and A and B fill it together for the other half? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 13. A tap can fill a tank in 6 hours. After half the tank is filled, three more similar taps are opened. What is the total time taken to fill the tank completely? A.  B.  C.  D.  15 min

question: 14. Three taps A, B and C can fill a tank in 12, 15 and 20 hours respectively. If A is open all the time and B and C are open for one hour each alternately, the tank will be full in: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 15. Three pipes A, B and C can fill a tank in 6 hours. After working at it together for 2 hours, C is closed and A and B can fill the remaining part in 7 hours. The number of hours taken by C alone to fill the tank is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Part filled in 2 hours = /26 = /13 Remaining part = (1 - /13 ) = /23 (A + B) 's 7 hour's work = /23 (A + B) 's 1 hour's work = /221 C's 1 hour's work = ( /16 - /221 ) = /114 C alone can fill the tank in 14 hours. ```

question: A boat can travel with a speed of 13 km/hr in still water. If the speed of the stream is 4 km/hr, find the time taken by the boat to go 68 km downstream. A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Speed downstream = (13 + 4) km/hr = 17 km/hr. Time taken to travel 68 km downstream = /6817 hrs = 4 hrs.

question: A man's speed with the current is 15 km/hr and the speed of the current is 2.5 km/hr. The man's speed against the current is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Man's rate in still water = (15 - 2.5) km/hr = 12.5 km/hr. Man's rate against the current = (12.5 - 2.5) km/hr = 10 km/hr.

question: A boat running upstream takes 8 hours 48 minutes to cover a certain distance, while it takes 4 hours to cover the same distance running downstream. What is the ratio between the speed of the boat and speed of the water current respectively? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.

question: In one hour, a boat goes 11 km/hr along the stream and  against the stream. The speed of the boat in still water (in km/hr) is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Speed in still water = /12 (11 + 5) kmph = 8 kmph.

question: A boat running downstream covers a distance of 16 km in 2 hours while for covering the same distance upstream, it takes 4 hours. What is the speed of the boat in still water? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Rate downstream = ( /162 ) kmph = 8 kmph. Rate upstream = ( /164 ) kmph = 4 kmph. Speed in still water = /12 (8 + 4) kmph = 6 kmph.

question: The speed of a boat in still water is 15 km/hr and the rate of current is 3 km/hr. The distance travelled downstream in 12 minutes is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Speed downstream = (15 + 3) kmph = 18 kmph. Distance travelled = /18 × 1260 km = 3.6 km.

question: A boat takes 90 minutes less to travel 36 miles downstream than to travel the same distance upstream. If the speed of the boat in still water is 10 mph, the speed of the stream is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the speed of the stream x mph. Then, Speed downstream = (10 + x) mph, Speed upstream = (10 - x) mph. /3610 - x - /3610 + x = 60 72x cdot 60 = 90 (100 - x^2) x^2 + 48x - 100 = 0 (x+ 50) (x - 2) = 0 x = 2 mph.

question: 9. A man can row at 5 kmph in still water. If the velocity of current is 1 kmph and it takes him 1 hour to row to a place and come back, how far is the place? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Speed downstream = (5 + 1) kmph = 6 kmph. Speed upstream = (5 - 1) kmph = 4 kmph. Let the required distance be x km. /x6 + /x4 = 1 2x + 3x = 12 5x = 12 x = 2.4 km.

question: 10. A boat covers a certain distance downstream in 1 hour, while it comes back in 1 /12 hours. If the speed of the stream be 3 kmph, what is the speed of the boat in still water? A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Let the speed of the boat in still water be x kmph. Then, Speed downstream = (x + 3) kmph, Speed upstream = (x - 3) kmph. / (x + 3) × 1 (x - 3) × 2 = /32 Rarrow 2x + 6 = 3x - 9 Rarrow x = 15 kmph.

question: 11. A boatman goes 2 km against the current of the stream in  and goes 1 km along the current in 10 minutes. How long will it take to go 5 km in stationary water? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Rate downstream = ( /110 × 60 ) km/hr = 6 km/hr. Rate upstream = 2 km/hr. Speed in still water = /12 (6 + 2) km/hr = 4 km/hr. Required time = ( /54 + /14 ) hrs = 1 hr 15 min.

question: 12. A man can row three-quarters of a kilometre against the stream in 11 /14 minutes and down the stream in 7 /12 minutes. The speed (in km/hr) of the man in still water is: A. 2 B. 3 C. 4 D. 5
explanation: We can write three-quarters of a kilometre as 750 metres, and 11 /14 minutes as 675 seconds. Rate upstream = /750675 × /99 = 10 m/sec. Rate downstream = /750450 × /33 = 5 m/sec. Rate in still water = /12 (10 + 5) = /152 m/sec. = /2518 m/sec. = /2518 × /185 km/hr. = 5 km/hr.

question: 13. Speed of a boat in standing water is 9 kmph and the speed of the stream is 1.5 kmph. A man rows to a place at a distance of 105 km and comes back to the starting point. The total time taken by him is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Speed upstream = 7.5 kmph. Speed downstream = 10.5 kmph. Total time taken = /1057.5 + /10510.5 hours = 24 hours.

question: 14. A man takes twice as long to row a distance against the stream as to row the same distance in favour of the stream. The ratio of the speed of the boat (in still water) and the stream is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let man's rate upstream be x kmph. Then, his rate downstream = 2x kmph. (Speed in still water) : (Speed of stream) = /2x + x2 : /2x - x2 = /3x2 : /x2 = 3 : 1.

question: 15. A man rows to a place 48 km distant and comes back in 14 hours. He finds that he can row 4 km with the stream in the same time as 3 km against the stream. The rate of the stream is: A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Suppose he move 4 km downstream in x hours. Then, Speed downstream = /4x km/hr. Speed upstream = /3x km/hr. /48 /4x + /48 /3x = 14 or x = 1. So, Speed downstream = 8 km/hr, Speed upstream = 6 km/hr. Rate of the stream = /12 (8 - 6) km/hr = 1 km/hr.

question: A vessel is filled with liquid, 3 parts of which are water and 5 parts syrup. How much of the mixture must be drawn off and replaced with water so that the mixture may be half water and half syrup? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: A can contains a mixture of two liquids A and B in the ratio 7 : 5. When 9 litres of mixture are drawn off and the can is filled with B, the ratio of A and B becomes 7 : 9. How many litres of liquid A was contained by the can initially? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: ```tex Suppose the can initially contains 7x and 5x of mixtures A and B respectively. Quantity of A in mixture = (7x - /712 × 9 ) litres = (7x - 21 ) litres. Quantity of B in mixture = (5x - /512 × 9 ) litres = (5x - 15 ) litres. /7x - 214 = /5x - 154 + 9. Therefore, 28x - 21 = 7 and 20x + 21 = 9. Thus, 252x - 189 = 140x + 147. Hence, 112x = 336. Therefore, x = 3. So, the can contained 21 litres of A. ```

question: 14. 8 litres are drawn from a cask full of wine and is then filled with water. This operation is performed three more t×. The ratio of the quantity of wine now in cask to that of water is 16 : 65. How much wine did the cask hold originally? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: In a 500 m /e, the ratio of the speeds of two contestants A and B is 3 : 4. A has a start of 1. Then, A wins by: A.  B.  C.  D.

question: 7. For an edition of 12,500 copies, the amount of Royalty paid by the publisher is Rs. 2,81,250. What should be the selling price of the book if the publisher desires a profit of 5%? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Clearly, S.P. of the book = 105% of C.P. Let the selling price of this edition (of 12500 books) be Rs. x. 15 : 105 = 281250 : x quad Rarrow quad x = Rs. /105 × 28125015 = Rs. 1968750. Therefore, S.P. of one book = Rs. /196875012500 = Rs. 157.50.

question: If for an edition of the book, the cost of paper is Rs. 56250, then find the promotion cost for this edition. A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Let the Promotion Cost for this edition be Rs. p. Then, 25 : 10 = 56250 : p quad Rarrow quad p = Rs. ( /56250 × 1025 ) = Rs. 22,500.

question: Which two expenditures together have central angle of 108°? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Central angle of 108° = /108360 × 100% = 30% of the total expenditure. Binding Cost + Transportation Cost = (20% + 10%) of the total expenditure = 30% of the total expenditure. Therefore, Binding Cost and Transportation Cost together have a central angle of 108°.

question: For which of the following pairs of years the total exports from the three Companies together are equal? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In 1993 = Rs. (30 + 80 + 60) crores = Rs. 170 crores. In 1994 = Rs. (60 + 40 + 90) crores = Rs. 190 crores. In 1995 = Rs. (40 + 60 + 120) crores = Rs. 220 crores. In 1996 = Rs. (70 + 60 + 90) crores = Rs. 220 crores. In 1997 = Rs. (100 + 80 + 60) crores = Rs. 240 crores. In 1998 = Rs. (50 + 100 + 80) crores = Rs. 230 crores. In 1999 = Rs. (120 + 140 + 100) crores = Rs. 360 crores. Clearly, the total exports of the three Companies X, Y and Z together are same during the years 1995 and 1996.

question: In which year was the difference between the exports from Companies X and Y the minimum? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In 1993 = Rs. (80 - 30) crores = Rs. 50 crores. In 1994 = Rs. (60 - 40) crores = Rs. 20 crores. In 1995 = Rs. (60 - 40) crores = Rs. 20 crores. In 1996 = Rs. (70 - 60) crores = Rs. 10 crores. In 1997 = Rs. (100 - 80) crores = Rs. 20 crores. In 1998 = Rs. (100 - 50) crores = Rs. 50 crores. In 1999 = Rs. (140 - 120) crores = Rs. 20 crores. Clearly, the difference is minimum in the year 1996.

question: What was the difference between the average exports of the three Companies in 1993 and the average exports in 1998? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Average exports of the three Companies X, Y and Z in 1993 = /13 × (30 + 80 + 60) crores = /1703 crores. Average exports of the three Companies X, Y and Z in 1998 = /13 × (50 + 100 + 80) crores = /2303 crores. Difference = /13 × (230 - 170) crores = /603 crores = 20 crores.

question: In how many of the given years, were the exports from Company Z more than the average annual exports over the given years? A. 2 B. 3 C. 4 D. 5
explanation: Average annual exports of Company Z during the given period = /17 × (60 + 90 + 120 + 90 + 60 + 80 + 100) = Rs. /6007 crores = Rs. 85.71 crores. From the analysis of graph, the exports of Company Z are more than the average annual exports of Company Z (i.e., Rs. 85.71 crores) during the years 1994, 1995, 1996 and 1999, i.e., during 4 of the given years.

question: Look at this series: 2, 1, (0.5) , (0.25) , ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple division series; each number is one-half of the previous number. In other terms to say, the number is divided by 2 successively to get the n result. 2.0 = 2 2/2 = 1 1/2 = 0.5 (0.5) /2 = 0.25 (0.25) /2 = 0.12 and so on.

question: Look at this series: 7, , 8, 11, 9, , ... What number should come n? A. 7 B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple alternating addition and subt/tion series. In the first pattern, 3 is added; in the second, 2 is subt/ted.

question: Look at this series: 36, 34, 30, 28, 24, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is an alternating number subt/tion series. First, 2 is subt/ted, then 4, then 2, and so on.

question: Look at this series: , 21, 23, , , 23, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this simple alternating subt/tion and addition series; 1 is subt/ted, then 2 is added, and so on.

question: Look at this series: , , 40, 40, , , ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this series, each number is repeated, then 13 is subt/ted to arrive at the n number.

question: Look at this series: , 9, , 11, , 13, , ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this alternating repetition series, the random number 21 is interpolated every other number into an otherwise simple addition series that increases by 2, beginning with the number 9.

question: Look at this series: 58, 52, 46, 40, 34, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple subt/tion series. Each number is 6 less than the previous number.

question: Look at this series: 3, 4, 7, 8, 11, 12, ... What number should come n? A. 7 B.  C.  D.
explanation: This alternating addition series begins with 3; then 1 is added to give 4; then 3 is added to give 7; then 1 is added, and so on.

question: Look at this series: 8, 22, 8, 28, 8, ... What number should come n? A. 9 B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple addition series with a random number, 8, interpolated as every other number. In the series, 6 is added to each number except 8, to arrive at the n number.

question: 10. Look at this series: 31, 29, 24, 22, 17, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple alternating subt/tion series, which subt/ts 2, then 5.

question: 11.~ Look at this series: 1.5, 2.3, 3.1, 3.9, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: Look at this series: 14, 28, 20, 40, 32, 64, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: .~ Look at this series: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple addition series. Each number increases by 2.

question: 14. Look at this series: 201, 202, 204, 207, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this addition series, 1 is added to the first number; 2 is added to the second number; 3 is added to the third number; 4 is added to the fourth number; and go on.

question: Look at this series: 544, 509, 474, 439, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple subt/tion series. Each number is 35 less than the previous number.

question: Look at this series: 80, 10, 70, 15, 60, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is an alternating addition and subt/tion series. In the first pattern, 10 is subt/ted from each number to arrive at the n. In the second, 5 is added to each number to arrive at the n.

question: 17. Look at this series: 2, 6, 18, 54, ... What number should come n? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is a simple multiplication series. Each number is 3 t× more than the previous number.

question: 18. Look at this series: 5.2, 4.8, 4.4, 4, ... What number should come n? A. 3 B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this simple subt/tion series, each number decreases by 0.4.

1. SCD, TEF, UGH, ____, WKL A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: There are two alphabetical series here. The first series is with the first letters only: STUVW. The second series involves the remaining letters: CD, EF, GH, IJ, KL.

question: B_2 CD, _____, BCD_4, B_5 CD, BC_6 D A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Because the letters are the same, concentrate on the number series, which is a simple 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 series, and follows each letter in order.

question: 3. FAG, GAF, HAI, IAH, ____ A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The middle letters are static, so concentrate on the first and third letters. The series involves an alphabetical order with a reversal of the letters. The first letters are in alphabetical order: F, G, H, I , J. The second and fourth segments are reversals of the first and third segments. The missing segment begins with a new letter.

question: 4. ELFA, GLHA, ILJA, ___, MLNA A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The second and forth letters in the series, L and A, are static. The first and third letters consist of an alphabetical order beginning with the letter E.

question: 5. CMM, EOO, GQQ, _____, KUU A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The first letters are in alphabetical order with a letter skipped in between each segment: C, E, G, I, K. The second and third letters are repeated; they are also in order with a skipped letter: M, O, Q, S, U.

question: 6. ZA_5, Y_4 B, XC_6, W_3 D, _____ A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: There are three series to look for here. The first letters are alphabetical in reverse: Z, Y, X, W, V. The second letters are in alphabetical order, beginning with A. The number series is as follows: 5, 4, 6, 3, 7.

question: 7. QPO, NML, KJI, _____, EDC A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This series consists of letters in a reverse alphabetical order.

question: 8. JAK, KBL, LCM, MDN, _____ A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This is an alternating series in alphabetical order. The middle letters follow the order ABCDE. The first and third letters are alphabetical beginning with J. The third letter is repeated as a first letter in each subsequent three-letter segment.

question: 9. BCB, DED, FGF, HIH, ___ A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: This series consists of a simple alphabetical order with the first two letters of all segments: B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K. The third letter of each segment is a repetition of the first letter.

question: P_5 QR, P_4 QS, P_3 QT, ___, P_1 QV A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The first two letters, PQ, are static. The third letter is in alphabetical order, beginning with R. The number series is in descending order beginning with 5.

question: 11. QAR, RAS, SAT, TAU, _____ A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this series, the third letter is repeated as the first letter of the n segment. The middle letter, A, remains static. The third letters are in alphabetical order, beginning with R.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Parsley, basil, and dill are types of herbs. Mayonnaise is not an herb.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Tulip, rose, and daisy are all types of flowers. A bud is not.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Loaf, sourdough, and pumpernickel are types of bread. A rye is not a bread type. Loaf - Bread that is shaped and baked in one piece and usually sliced before being eaten. Pumpernickel - Dark, dense German bread made from coarsely ground whole-grain rye. Sourdough - Leaven for making bread, consisting of fermenting dough, typically that over from a previous batch. Rye - A wheatlike cereal plant that tolerates poor soils and low temperatures.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The guitar, violin, and cello are stringed instruments; the flute is a wind instrument.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Dodge, duck, and avoid are all synonyms meaning evade. Flee means to run away from.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A branch, leaf, and root are all parts of a tree. The dirt underneath is not a part of the tree.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Freeway, interstate, and expressway are all high-speed highways; a street is for low-speed traffic.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Heading, body, and closing are all parts of a letter; the letter is the whole, not a part.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The yarn, twine, and cord are all used for tying. The tape is not used in the same way.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A leopard, cougar, and lion all belong to the cat family; an elephant does not.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The couch, table, and chair are pieces of furniture; the rug is not.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The cornea, retina, and pupil are all parts of the eye.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The noun, preposition, and adverb are classes of words that make up a sentence. Punctuation belongs in a sentence, but punctuation is not a class of word.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: An index, glossary, and chapter are all parts of a book. Choice a does not belong because the book is the whole, not a part.

question: Which word does NOT belong with the others? A. B.  C.  D.

question: desert A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A desert is an arid t/t of land. Not all deserts are flat (choice d) . Not all deserts have cacti or oases (choices a and c) .

question: book A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The necessary part of a book is its pages; there is no book without pages. Not all books are fiction (choice a) , and not all books have pictures (choice c) . Learning (choice d) may or may not take place with a book

question: language A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Words are a necessary part of language. Slang is not necessary to language (choice b) . Not all languages are written (choice c) . Not all languages are spoken (choice a) .

question: school A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Without students, a school cannot exist; therefore, students are the essential part of schools. The other choices may be related, but they are not essential.

question: monopoly A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: The essential part of a monopoly is that it involves exclusive ownership or control.

question: guitar A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A guitar does not exist without strings, so strings are an essential part of a guitar. A band is not necessary to a guitar (choice a) . Guitar playing can be learned without a teacher (choice b) . Songs are byproducts of a guitar (choice c) .

question: 13. lning A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Lning is produced from a discharge of electricity, so electricity is essential. Thunder and rain are not essential to the production of lning (choices b and d) . Brness may be a byproduct of lning, but it is not essential (choice c) .

question: Odometer is to mileage as compass is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: An odometer is an instrument used to measure mileage. A compass is an instrument used to determine direction. Choices a, b, and c are incorrect because none is an instrument.

question: Marathon is to /e as hibernation is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A marathon is a long /e and hibernation is a lengthy period of sleep. The answer is not choice a or b because even though a bear and winter are related to hibernation, neither completes the analogy. (Choice c) is incorrect because sleep and dream are not synonymous.

question: Window is to pane as book is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A window is made up of panes, and a book is made up of pages. The answer is not (choice a) because a novel is a type of book. The answer is not (choice b) because glass has no relationship to a book. (Choice c) is incorrect because a cover is only one part of a book; a book is not made up of covers.

question: Cup is to coffee as bowl is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Coffee goes into a cup and soup goes into a bowl. Choices a and c are incorrect because they are other utensils. The answer is not choice d because the word food is too general.

question: Yard is to inch as quart is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A yard is a larger measure than an inch (a yard contains 36 inches) . A quart is a larger measure than an ounce (a quart contains 32 ounces) . Gallon (choice a) is incorrect because it is larger than a quart. Choices c and d are incorrect because they are not units of measurement.

question: Elated is to despondent as enlened is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Elated is the opposite of despondent; enlened is the opposite of ignorant.

question: Optimist is to cheerful as pessimist is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: An optimist is a person whose outlook is cheerful. A pessimist is a person whose outlook is gloomy. The answer is not (choice b) because a pessimist does not have to be mean. (Choices c) and d are incorrect because neither adjective describes the outlook of a pessimist.

question: Reptile is to lizard as flower is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A lizard is a type of reptile; a daisy is a type of flower. Choices a and b are incorrect because a petal and a stem are parts of a flower, not types of flowers. (Choice d) is incorrect because an alligator is another type of reptile, not a type of flower.

question: Play is to actor as concert is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: An actor performs in a play. A musician performs at a concert. Choices a, c, and d are incorrect because none is people who perform.

question: Sponge is to porous as rubber is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A sponge is a porous material. Rubber is an elastic material. (Choice a) is incorrect because rubber would not generally be referred to as massive. The answer is not (choice b) because even though rubber is a solid, its most noticeable cha/teristic is its elasticity. Choice d is incorrect because rubber has flexibility.

question: 11. Careful is to cautious as boastful is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Careful and cautious are synonyms (they mean the same thing) . Boastful and arrogant are also synonyms. The answer is not (choice b) because humble means the opposite of boastful. The answer is not choice c or d because neither means the same as boastful.

question: Pen is to poet as needle is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A pen is a tool used by a poet. A needle is a tool used by a tailor. The answer is not choice a, b, or c because none is a person and therefore cannot complete the analogy.

question: Secretly is to openly as silently is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Secretly is the opposite of openly, and silently is the opposite of noisily. Choices a and b are clearly not the opposites of silently. (Choice d) means the same thing as silently.

question: Embarrassed is to humiliated as frened is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: If someone has been humiliated, they have been greatly embarrassed. If someone is terrified, they are remely frened. The answer is not choice b because an agitated person is not necessarily frened. Choices c and d are incorrect because neither word expresses a state of being frened.

question: Pride is to lion as shoal is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A group of lions is called a pride. A group of fish swim in a shoal. Teacher (choice a) and student (choice b) refer to another meaning of the word school. The answer is not (choice c) because self-respect has no obvious relationship to this particular meaning of school.

question: Artist is to painting as senator is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: An artist makes paintings; a senator makes laws. The answer is not choice a because an attorney does not make laws and a senator is not an attorney. Choice c is incorrect because a senator is not a politician. Constituents (choice d) is also incorrect because a senator serves his or her constituents.

question: Exercise is to gym as eating is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: A gym is a place where people exercise. A restaurant is a place where people eat. Food (choice a) is not the answer because it is something people eat, not a place or location where they eat. The answer is not choice b or c because neither represents a place where people eat.

question: Candid is to indirect as honest is to A.  B.  C.  D. un
explanation: Candid and indirect refer to opposing traits. Honest and untruthful refer to opposing traits. The answer is not choice a because frank means the same thing as candid. Wicked (choice b) is incorrect because even though it refers to a negative trait, it does not mean the opposite of honest. (Choice c) is incorrect because truthful and honest mean the same thing.

question: Guide is to direct as reduce is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Guide and direct are synonyms, and reduce and decrease are synonyms. The answer is not choice b or d because neither means the same as reduce. (Choice c) is incorrect because increase is the opposite of reduce.

question: Oar is to rowboat as foot is to A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: An oar puts a rowboat into motion. A foot puts a skateboard into motion. The answer is not choice a because running is not an object that is put into motion by a foot. Sneaker (choice b) is incorrect because it is something worn on a foot. Jumping (choice d) is incorrect because although you do need feet to jump, jumping is not an object that is put into motion by means of a foot.

question: 1. Here are some words translated from an artificial language. gorblflur means fan belt pixngorbl means ceiling fan arthtusl means tile roof Which word could mean "ceiling tile"? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Gorbl means fan; flur means belt; pixn means ceiling; arth means tile; and tusl means roof. Therefore, pixnarth is the correct choice.

question: 2. Here are some words translated from an artificial language.~ ithapllesh means cloudburst.~ itsrenchoch means pinball.~ itresbosrench means ninepin.~ Which word could mean "cloud nine"? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: Hapl means cloud; lesh means burst; srench means pin; och means ball; and resbo means nine. Leshsrench (choice a) doesn't contain any of the words needed for cloud nine. We know that och means ball, so that rules out choices b and c. When you combine hapl (cloud) with resbo (nine) , you get the correct answer

question: 3. Here are some words translated from an artificial language. agnoscrenia means poisonous spider delanocrenia means poisonous snake agnosdeery means brown spider Which word could mean "black widow spider"? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: In this language, the noun appears first and the adjectives follow. Since agnos means spider and should appear first, choices a and d can be ruled out. Choice b can be ruled out because delano means snake.

question: Here are some words translated from an artificial language.~ itmoolokarn means blue sky.~ itwilkospadi means bicycle /e.~ itmoolowilko means blue bicycle.~ Which word could mean "/ecar"? A.  B.  C.  D.
explanation: From itwilkospadi, you can determine that itwilko means bicycle and itspadi means /e. Therefore, the first part of the word that means /ecar should begin with itspadi. That limits your choices to b and d. Choice b, itspadiwilko, is incorrect because we have already determined that wilko means bicycle. Therefore, the answer must be choice d, itspadivolo.
