The amount collected by way of surcharge may be adequate to fund these drought relief programmes.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Since a surcharge has been levied to fund drought relief programmes, it follows that the Government does not have sufficient money for the same. So, I is implicit. Besides, the percentage of surcharge must have been decided after studying the expected inflow in relation to amount of funds required. So, II is also implicit.

question: 12. Statement: Detergents should be used to clean clothes. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Detergents form more lather. item Detergents help to dislodge grease and dirt. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Nothing is mentioned about lather formation by the detergent. So, I is not implicit. Also, detergents should be used as they clean clothes better and more easily. So, II is implicit.

question: Statement: It will be a substantial achievement in the field of education if one provides one school for every village in our country and enforce attendance. Assumptions:  eginflalign* & 1. Children in villages do not attend school regularly.& & 2. Providing school to every village is desirable.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The statement lays stress on enforcing attendance. This implies that children in villages do not attend school regularly. So, I is implicit. Besides, the statement calls 'one school for every village' a 'substantial achievement'. So, II is also implicit.

question: 14. Statement: The government has decided to disinvest large chunk of its equity in select public sector undertakings for a better fiscal management. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The amount generated out of the disinvestment process may reduce substantially the mounting fiscal deficits. item There will be enough demand in the market for the shares of these undertakings. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The fact given in I directly follows from the phrase '..... for a better fiscal management' in the statement. So, I is implicit. However, the public response to the new policy cannot be ascertained. So, II is not implicit.

question: 15. Statement: Never before such a lucid book was available on the topic. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item Some other books were available on this topic. item You can write lucid books on very few topics. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: It follows from the statement that books on this topic were available before also but they were not 'lucid'. So, I is implicit. But a general comment as II cannot be made from the given statement. So, II is not implicit.

question: 16. Statement: Please do not use lift while going down - an instruction on the top floor of a five-storey building. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item While going down, the lift is unable to carry any load. item Provision of lift is a matter of facility and not of r. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement requests people not to use lift while moving down. This implies that the lift may be used to move up and the request has been made so that more people can use the lift for ascending which would otherwise cause more physical stress than going down the stairs. So, only II is implicit.

question: 17. Statement: "I have not received telephone bills for nine months inspite of several complaints" - A telephone customer's letter to the editor of a daily Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Every customer has a r to get bills regularly from the telephone company. item The customer's complaints point to defect in the services which are expected to be corrected. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The customer's eagerness to get the bills makes I implicit. Besides, the customer has written to the editor to bring the malfunctioning of the department to public notice. So, II is also implicit.

question: 18. Statement: "This drink can be had either as it is, or after adding ice to it." - An advertisement. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item People differ in their preferences. item Some people will get att/ted to the drink as it can be had as it is. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The advertisement tells the different ways in which the drink can be had. This means that different people prefer to have it in a different way and that some people would prefer it only because it can be taken in a particular manner. So, both I and II are implicit.

question: 19. Statement: Government has permitted unaided colleges to increase their fees. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Unaided colleges are in financial difficulties. item Aided colleges do not need to increase fees. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Unaided colleges have been allowed to increase their fees. This means that they are in financial difficulties. So, I is implicit. Nothing is mentioned about the aided colleges. So, II is not implicit.

question: Statement: Be humble even after being victorious. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Many people are humble after being victorious. item Generally people are not humble. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Clearly, nothing is mentioned about the nature of the people. So, I is not implicit. Also, the statement gives an advice of being humble even after being victorious. This means that generally people are not humble. So, II is implicit.

question: 21. Statement: The government has decided to pay compensation to the tune of Rs. 1 lakh to the family members of those who are killed in railway accidents. Assumptions:  eginenumerate item The government has enough funds to meet the expenses due to compensation. item There may be reduction in incidents of railway accidents in near future. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Clearly, the amount of compensation must have been decided keeping in mind the monetary position of the Government. So, I is implicit. However, nothing can be said about the frequency of railway accidents in future. So, II is not implicit.

question: 22. Statement: Films have become indispensable for the entertainment of people. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Films are the only media of entertainment. item People enjoy films. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Films are indispensable does not mean that they are the only means of entertainment. So, I is not implicit. Clearly, II follows from the statement. So, it is implicit.

question: 23. Statement: Of all the newspapers published in Mumbai, readership of the "T×" is the largest in the Metropolis. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item 'T×' is not popular in mofussil areas. item 'T×' has the popular feature of cartoons on burning social and political issues. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: Neither I nor II is implicit
explanation: Neither the volume of readership of the 'T×' in areas other than the Metropolis nor the reason for its huge acclamation is evident from the statement. So, neither I nor II is implicit.

question: 24. Statement: Apart from the entertainment value of television, its educational value cannot be ignored. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item People take television to be a means of entertainment only. item The educational value of television is not realised properly. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The statement makes the first assumption clear though educational value is not to be ignored. So, I is implicit. That the educational value must not be ignored also shows that educational value is not realised properly. So, II is also implicit.

question: 25. Statement: Children are influenced more by their teachers nowadays. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The children consider teachers as their models. item A large amount of children's time is spent in school. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Clearly, it is because children consider teachers as their model that they are more influenced by them. So, I is implicit. It is not necessary that the children are influenced by teachers because they spend much time in school. So, II is not implicit.

question: 26. Statement: You know that your suit is excellent when people ask about your tailor who tailored the suit. Assumptions:  eginflalign* & 1. People do not ask about your tailor if your suit is not good.& & 2. The people want to know the criterion of an excellent suit.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement mentions that if the people ask about the tailor, your suit is good. This means that people ask only in the situation when the thing is good. So, I is implicit. The criteria of an excellent suit is not mentioned. So, II is not implicit.

question: Statement: His recent investment in the shares of Company A is only a gamble. Assumptions:  eginflalign* & 1. He may incur loss on his investment.& & 2. He may gain from his investment.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: Either I or II is implicit
explanation: The use of the world 'gamble' indicates that he may either gain or lose in the deal.

question: 28. Statement: Why don't you go to the court if the employer does not pay you the Provident Fund contribution? Assumptions:  eginflalign* & 1. Courts can intervene in matters of dispute between employer and employees.& & 2. It is obligatory for the employer to pay the Provident Fund contribution to the employees.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, the statement encourages one to go to court to get his Provident Fund from his employer. This implies that the issue comes under the jurisdiction of courts and that it is the r of the employee to claim his Provident Fund. So, both I and II are implicit.

question: 29.~ Statement: 'Double your money in five months.' - An advertisement.~ Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The assurance is not genuine.~ item People want their money to grow. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The truth or falsity of the promise made in the statement cannot be judged. So, I is not implicit. Since the concerned firm advertises with the assurance that money can be doubled quickly by investing with it, so II is implicit.

question: Statement: The education of a student at collegiate level, not taking into account maintenance expenses, costs four hundred rupees a year. Collegiate education is thus drawing heavily upon the national resources of an impoverished community. So college education should be restricted to a brilliant few. Assumptions:  eginenumerate item Our resources are very limited. item Only a few students should be admitted to the colleges. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The use of the words 'impoverished community' in the statement makes I implicit while the phrase 'college education should be restricted to a brilliant few' makes II implicit.

question: 31. Statement: A's advice to B - "Go to Jammu via Amritsar - the shortest route". Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item B wishes to go to Jammu. item A gives advice to everybody. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: A has advised B the route to Jammu. This means that B wishes to go to Jammu. So, I is implicit. The statement mentions only A's advice to B. So, II is not implicit.

question: 32.~ Statement: All existing inequalities can be reduced, if not utterly eradicated, by action of governments or by revolutionary change of government.~ Assumptions:  eginenumerate item Inequality is a man-made phenomenon. item No person would voluntarily part with what he possesses. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Since inequality can be reduced, it means that it is not natural but created. So, I is implicit. Nothing is mentioned about people's response. So, II is not implicit.

question: 33.~ Statement: The campaign of 'Keep your city clean' started by the Civil Council did not evoke any response from the citizens.~ Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item People do not desire to keep their city clean. item The Civil Council has failed in its campaign. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: According to the statement, the campaign did not get any response from citizens. This means that people are not interested in keeping the city clean and the campaign has failed. So, both I and II are implicit.

question: 34.~ Statement: The district administration has issued a circular to all the farmers under its jurisdiction advising them for not using pesticides indiscriminately as it may pollute the ground water.~ Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item People may stop using ground water if the farmers continue to use pesticides indiscriminately. item Farmers may refrain from using pesticides indiscriminately. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The district administration has issued a circular to make the farmers aware of hazards that indiscriminate use of pesticides poses to ground water and plead them to refrain from the same. So, II is implicit. However, I cannot be assumed from the given statement and so it is not implicit.

question: 35. Statement: The coffee powder of company X is quite better in taste than the much advertised coffee of company Y. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item If your product is not good, you spend more on advertisement. item Some people are tempted to buy a product by the advertisement. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Since the statement holds the product of company X more superior in quality than that of Y which spends more on advertisement, so I is not implicit. According to the statement, the product of company Y is more known because of more advertisement. So, II is implicit.

question: 36. Statement: Of all the radio sets manufactured in India, the 'X' brand has the largest sale. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The sale of all the radio sets manufactured in India is known. item The manufacturing of no other radio set in India is as large as 'X' brand radio. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Clearly, the comparison could not be made without knowing the sale of all the radio sets. So, I is implicit. The statement mentions only that the sale is largest and nothing is mentioned about the manufacture. So, II is not implicit.

question: 37. Statement: "You should not grant him leave in this week due to exigency of work." - A supervisor advises the administrative officer. Assumptions:  eginenumerate item Request for leave can be turned down also. item The supervisor has reviewed the work required to be done during the said period. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The advice is given to turn down the request for leave. So, I is implicit. The mention of the 'exigency of work' makes II implicit.

question: Statement: Like a mad man, I decided to follow him. Assumptions:  eginenumerate item I am not a mad man. item I am a mad man. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: Either I or II is implicit
explanation: The words 'Like a mad man' show that either a person is really mad or he is not mad but acted like mad. So, either I or II is implicit.

question: 39. Statement: The first step in treating addicts is to re-establish their lost ties, for which a continuous personal attention should be paid to the addicts under treatment. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Addicts under treatment respond better when shown personal interest. item Addiction and strained relationships are intimately connected. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, treatment of addiction requires personal attention as the first step. So, I is implicit. Also, since intimacy and personal attention are required to treat addicts, it implies that addiction arises out of frustration due to strained relationships. So, II is also implicit.

question: Statement: Beware of dogs, our dogs do not bark, but they are trained to distinguish between genuine guests and intruders. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Barking dogs bite rarely. item Our dogs could be dangerous for intruders. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement clearly warns the visitors to beware of dogs as they are trained to welcome the guests and intruders differently. So, II is implicit. I is vague and hence, it is not implicit.

question: 41. Statement: Because of the large number of potholes in road X, reaching airport in time has become difficult. Assumptions:  eginflalign* & 1. Reaching airport in time may not be always necessary.& & 2. There is no other convenient road to the airport.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement presents the issue of 'not reaching airport in time' as a problem. This means that reaching airport in time is necessary. So, I is not implicit. Besides, it is mentioned that reaching airport in time has become difficult due to large number of potholes in road X. This implies that road X is the only possible way. So, II is implicit.

question: 42. Statement: Safety and health p/tices in many Indian companies are well below the international standards. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item International standards of health and safety are ideal and unrealistic. item Indian organizations do not consider safety and health management as their prime social responsibility. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement talks about the safety and health p/tices in Indian companies being far below international standards. It is clearly a criticism of Indian organizations not paying considerable attention to these aspects. So, II is implicit. The international standards demand perfection and are, in no way, non-achievable. So, I is not implicit.

question: 43. Statement: Greater public participation results in good civic governance. - Statement of Municipal Commissioner of City A. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The municipal office is not competent to effect good civic administration. item Good civic governance is a matter of collective will and effort of the people and administration. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement stresses on the fact that though civic governance is the task of the municipal body, but all the tasks done come out to be more fruitful if the general public lends a helping hand in the same. So, only II is implicit.

question: 44. Statement: The regulatory authority has set up a review committee to find out the reasons for unstable stock prices. Assumptions:  eginenumerate[label= oman*.] item The investors may regain confidence in stock market by this decision. item The review committee has the expertise to find out the causes for volatility in the stock market. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, I mentions the aim for which the step talked about in the statement, has been undertaken while II mentions the essential requirement for it. So, both I and II are implicit.

question: 45. Statement: Please note that the company will provide accommodation to only outside candidates if selected.' - A condition in an advertisement. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!<2>] item The local candidates would be having some other arrangement for their stay. item The company plans to select only local candidates. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement mentions that the company intends to provide accommodation only to outside candidates. This means that local candidates would have to arrange accommodation on their own and that the company may select local as well as outside candidates. Thus, only I is implicit.

question: 46. Statement: Cases of food poisoning due to consumption of liquor are increasing in rural areas. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Percentage of people consuming liquor is more in rural areas. item There are many unauthorized spurious liquor shops in the rural areas. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The statement talks of number of cases of food poisoning due to consumption of liquor and not of the number of cases consuming liquor. So, I is not implicit. Besides, the statement indicates that people in rural areas are getting spurious or low-grade liquor and no check is being kept on shops selling liquor there. So, II is implicit.

question: 47. Statement: Lack of stimulation in the first four or five years of life can have adverse consequences. Assumptions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item A great part of the development of observed intelligence occurs in the earliest years of life. item 50 percent of the measurable intelligence at age of 17 is already predictable by the age of four. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: The lacking in first four or five years can be adverse because it is the main period of development. So, I is implicit. Since nothing is mentioned about the predictability of intelligence, II is not implicit.

question: 48. Statement: The X-Airlines has decided to increase the passenger fare by 15 percent with immediate effect. Assumptions:  eginflalign* & 1. The demand for seats of X-Airlines may remain unchanged even after the hike of fare.& & 2. Other airline companies may also hike the passenger fares.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
explanation: Clearly, such decisions are taken only after making sure that it will not affect the company's business adversely. So, I is implicit. However, the impact of this increase on other airlines cannot be ascertained. So, II is not implicit.

question: 49. Statement: Shalini made an application to the bank for a loan of Rs. 1,80,000 by mortgaging her house to the bank and promised to repay it within five years. Assumptions:  eginenumerate item The bank has a p/tice of granting loans for Rs. 1,00,000 and above. item The bank accepts house as collateral security against such loans. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
options: Either I or II is implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The fact that Shalini has applied for a loan of Rs. 1,80,000 implies that the bank can grant a loan above Rs. 1,00,000. So, I is implicit. II also follows directly from the statement and so is implicit.

question: Statement: A large number of people in ward X of the city are diagnosed to be suffering from a fatal malaria type. Courses of Action:  eginflalign* & 1. The city municipal authority should take immediate steps to carry out ensive fumigation in ward X.& & 2. The people in the area should be advised to take steps to avoid mosquito bites.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, prevention from mosquitoes and elimination of mosquitoes are two ways to prevent malaria. So, both the courses follow.

question: Statement: Severe drought is reported to have set in several parts of the country. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Government should immediately make arrangement for providing financial assistance to those affected. item Food, water and fodder should immediately be sent to all these areas to save the people and cattle. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: In the break-out of a natural calamity, the basic duty of the government becomes to provide the basic amenities essential to save the lives of people and cattle. Providing financial assistance to all would put undue burden on the country's resources. So, only II follows.

question: Statement: Since its launching in 1981, Vayudoot has so far accumulated losses amounting to Rs 153 crore. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Vayudoot should be directed to reduce wasteful expenditure and to increase passenger fare. item An amount of about Rs 300 crore should be provided to Vayudoot to make the airliner economically viable. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, for better economic gain, losses should be reduced and income increased. So, only course I follows.

question: Statement: Exporters in the capital are alleging that commercial banks are violating a Reserve Bank of India directive to operate a post shipment export credit denominated in foreign currency at international rates from January this year. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The officers concerned in the commercial banks are to be suspended. item The RBI should be asked to stop giving such directives to commercial banks. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The statement mentions that the commercial banks violate a directive issued by the RBI. The remedy is only to make the banks implement the Act. So, none of the courses follows.

question: Statement: A large number of people die every year due to drinking polluted water during the summer. Courses of Action:  eginflalign* & 1. The government should make adequate arrangements to provide safe drinking water to all its citizens.& & 2. The people should be educated about the dangers of drinking polluted water.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The situation demands creating awareness among people about the dangers of drinking polluted water so that they themselves refrain from the same, and at the same time taking steps to provide safe drinking water. So, both the courses follow.

question: Statement: Most of those who study in premier engineering colleges in India migrate to developed nations for better prospects in their professional pursuits. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item All the students joining these colleges should be asked to sign a bond at the time of admission to the effect that they will remain in India at least for ten years after they complete education. item All those students who desire to settle in the developed nations should be asked to pay entire cost of their education which the government subsidises. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, no student can be bound to live and work in the country against his wish. So, I does not follow. However, it is quite r to recover the ra benefits awarded to students if they do not serve their own country. So, II follows.

question: 7. Statement: There is an unprecedented increase in migration of villagers to urban areas as repeated crop failure has put them into precarious financial situation. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The villagers should be provided with alternate source of income in their villages which will make them stay put. item The migrated villagers should be provided with jobs in the urban areas to help them survive. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, increased migration would add to the burden on city's infrastructure. So, attempts should be made to make the villagers feel comfortable in the villages itself. So, only course I follows.

question: 8. Statement: As stated in the recent census report, the female to male ratio is alarmingly low. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The government should conduct another census to verify the results. item The government should immediately issue orders to all the departments to encourage people to improve the ratio. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: A census is always conducted with the utmost precision, leaving chances of only negligible differences. So, I does not follow. Further, the ratio can be improved by creating awareness among the masses and abolishing female foeticide. Thus, only course II follows.

question: Statement: Four districts in State A have been experiencing severe drought for the last three years resulting into exodus of people from these districts. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The government should immediately start food for work programme in the district to put a halt to the exodus. item The government should make since efforts to provide drinking/potable water to these districts endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The exodus can be stopped by providing the people conditions conducive to living. So, both the courses follow.

question: Statement: If the retired Professors of the same Institutes are also invited to deliberate on restructuring of the organisation, their contribution may be beneficial to the Institute. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Management may seek opinion of the employees before calling retired professors. item Management should involve experienced people for the systematic restructuring of the organisation. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the statement stresses that the contribution of retired Professors shall be beneficial. This means that these people's experience regarding working of the organisation is helpful. So, only course II follows.

question: 11. Statement: The sale of a particular product has gone down considerably causing great concern to the company. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item The company should make a proper study of rival products in the market. item The price of the product should be reduced and quality improved. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, a study of rival products in the market will help assess the cause for the lowering down of sales and then a suitable action can be taken. Thus, only I follow.

question: 12. Statement: The Asian Development Bank has approved a 285 million loan to finance a project to construct coal ports by Paradip and Madras Port Trusts. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item India should use financial assistance from other international financial organisations to develop such ports in other places. item India should not seek such financial assistance from the international financial agencies. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, such projects shall be an asset and a source of income to the country later on. So, course I shall follow.

question: 13.~ Statement: Doordarshan is concerned about the quality of its programmes particularly in view of stiff competition it is facing from STAR and other satellite TV channels and is contemplating various measures to att/t talent for its programmes.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item In an effort to att/t talent, the Doordarshan has decided to revise its fee structure for the artists. item The fee structure should not be revised until other electronic media also revise it. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the decision to revise its fee structure for artists is taken by Doordarshan as a remedy to the challenging problem that had arisen before it. It cannot wait till other media take action. So, only course I follows.

question: 14. Statement: The Minister said that the teachers are still not familiarised with the need, importance and meaning of population education in the higher education system. They are not even clearly aware about their role and responsibilities in the population education programme. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item Population education programme should be included in the college curriculum. item Orientation programme should be conducted for teachers on population education. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the statement stresses on teachers' lack of awareness and knowledge in population education and as such the best remedy would be to guide them in this field through orientation programmes. So, only course II follows.

question: Statement: A recent study shows that children below five die in the cities of the developing countries mainly from diarrhoea and parasitic intestinal worms. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Governments of the developing countries should take adequate measures to improve the hygienic conditions in the cities. item Children below five years in the cities of the developing countries need to be kept under periodic medical check-up. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, the two diseases mentioned are caused by unhygienic conditions. So, improving the hygienic conditions is a step towards their eradication. Also, periodic medical check-up will help timely detection of the disease and hence a proper treatment. So, both I and II follow.

question: Statement: The kharif crops have been affected by the insects for consecutive three years in the district and the farmers harvested less than fifty percent of produce during these years. Courses of Action:  eginflalign* & 1. The farmers should seek measures to control the attack of insects to protect their crops n year.& & 2. The Government should increase the support price of kharif crops considerably to protect the economic interests of farmers.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, the problem demands taking ra care and adequate precautions to protect crops from insects and ending help to farmers to prevent them from incurring huge losses. Thus, both the courses follow.

question: 17.~ Statement: The car dealer found that there was a tremendous response for the new XYZ's car-booking with long queues of people complaining about the duration of business hours and arrangements.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item People should make their arrangement of lunch and snacks while going for car XYZ's booking and be ready to spend several hours.~ item Arrangement should be made for more booking desks and increased business hours to serve more people in less time. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Seeing the tremendous response, the dealer must make suitable arrangements and deploy more personnel to take care of customers so that they don't have to wait excessively long for booking. So, only course II follows.

question: 18.~ Statement: The State Government has decided to declare 'Kala Azar' as a notifiable disease under the Epidemics Act. Family members or neighbours of the patient are liable to be punished in case they did not inform the State authorities.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Efforts should be made to effectively implement the Act. item The cases of punishment should be propagated through mass media so that more people become aware of the stern actions. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The Act is aimed at eradication of the disease and so it needs to be proclaimed and promoted. So, both the courses follow.

question: 19. Statement: The Chairman stressed the need for making education system more flexible and regretted that the curriculum has not been revised in keeping with the pace of the changes taking place. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item Curriculum should be reviewed and revised periodically. item System of education should be made more flexible. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, the situation demands making the education system more flexible and changing it periodically according to the needs of the time. So, both the courses follow.

question: 20. Statement: The Central Bureau of Investigation receives the complaint of an officer taking bribe to do the duty he is supposed to. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item CBI should try to catch the officer red-handed and then take a strict action against him. item CBI should wait for some more complaints about the officer to be sure about the matter. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, one complaint is enough for a wrong doing. This should be confirmed by catching the guilty red-handed and then strict action taken against him. So, only course I follows.

question: 21. Statement: The Indian electronic component industry venturing into the West European markets faces tough competition from the Japanese. Courses of Action:  eginflalign* & 1. India should search for other international markets for its products.& & 2. India should improve the quality of the electronic components to compete with the Japanese in capturing these markets.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: An escapist's attitude does not help much. The need is to compete and emerge successful. So, only course II follows.

question: 22. Statement: Orissa and Andhra Pradesh have agreed in principle to set up a joint control board for better control, management and productivity of several inter-state multipurpose projects. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Other neighbouring states should set up such control boards. item The proposed control board should not be allowed to function as such joint boards are always ineffective. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The effectiveness of such Control Boards is established by the fact that Orissa and A.P. have agreed to it for better control of its multipurpose projects. So, only course I follows.

question: 23. Statement: The Government has decided not to provide financial support to voluntary organisations from n Five Year Plan and has communicated that all such organisations should raise funds to meet their financial needs. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item Voluntary organisations should collaborate with foreign agencies. item They should explore other sources of financial support. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The problem arising is shortage of funds. So, alternative sources of financial support need to be worked out first. Thus, only course II follows.

question: Statement: The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased. Courses of Action:  eginflalign* & 1. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose high import duty on these fruits, even if these are not of good quality.& & 2. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would be increased.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.

question: 25. Statement: There has been an unprecedented increase in the number of successful candidates in this year's School Leaving Certificate Examination. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The government should make arrangements to increase number of seats of intermediate courses in existing colleges. item The government should take active steps to open new colleges to accommodate all these successful candidates. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The increase may not be a permanent one. So, it's better not to open new colleges but increase seats in the existing colleges. So, only I follow.

question: 26. Statement: On an average, about twenty people are run over by trains and die every day while crossing the railway t/ks through the level crossing. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item The railway authorities should be instructed to close all the level crossings. item Those who are found crossing the t/ks, when the gates are closed, should be fined heavily. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The accidents can clearly be prevented by barring people from crossing the t/ks when the gates are closed, So, only II follows.

question: 27.~ Statement: Majority of the students in many schools do not pass in the final examination.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item These schools should be closed down as these have become unproductive. item The teachers of these schools should immediately be retrenched. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the situation demands that efforts be made to remove the lackenings in the present system of education and adequate measures be taken to improve the performance of students. Harsh measures as those given in I and II, won't help. So, none of the given courses follows.

question: 28. Statement: In spite of the Principal's repeated warnings, a child was caught exploding c/kers secretly in the school. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item All the c/kers should be taken away from the child and he should be threatened not to do it again. item The child should be severely punished for his wrong act. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Since the act has been repeated despite various warnings, so course I would only be another warning and would not help. Severe punishment to set example for him and others is inevitable. Thus, course II shall follow.

question: Statement: It is necessary to adopt suitable measures to prevent repetition of bad debts by learning from the past experiences of mounting non-performing assets of banks. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Before granting loan to customers their eligibility for loan should be evaluated strictly. item To ensure the payment of instalments of loan, the work, for which loan was granted, should be supervised minutely on regular basis. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: To ensure that debts taken are repaid promptly, the customer's requirements and future prospects ought to be studied and their work constantly checked. Thus, both the courses follow.

question: 30. Statement: A very large number of students have failed in the final high school examination due to faulty questions in one of the subjects. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item All the students who have failed in the subject should be allowed to take supplementary examination. item All those who are responsible for the error should be suspended and an enquiry should be initiated to find out the facts. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: There being faulty questions in the examination paper is a blunder on the part of school management and students should not be made to suffer on account of this. Thus, a re-test should be organised for the students and those responsible for the error be penalised to prevent reoccurrence of such mistake in the future. Hence, both the courses follow.

question: 31. Statement: A large number of engineering graduates in the country are not in a position to have gainful employment at present and the number of such engineers is likely to grow in the future. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The government should launch att/tive employment generation schemes and encourage these graduates to opt for such schemes to use their expertise and knowledge effectively. item This happened due to proliferation of engineering colleges in the country and thereby lowered the quality of the engineering graduates. Those colleges which are not equipped to impart quality education should be closed down. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The emphasis should be not on the Government putting all the engineering graduates to jobs but on the colleges producing not 'degree-holders' but real technical minds which could compete well for gainful employment. So, only course II follows.

question: 32.~ Statement: Every year, at the beginning or at the end of the monsoons, we have some cases of conjunctivitis, but this year, it seems to be a major epidemic, witnessed after nearly four years.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Precautionary measures should be taken after every four years to check this epidemic. item People should be advised to drink boiled water during rainy season. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The disease occurs at the end of monsoons every year. So, precautionary measures every four years shall not help. The second course of action shall be a preventive measure. So, only course II follows.

question: 33.~ Statement: Every year large number of devotees dies due to severe cold on their way to the shrine located at the top of the mountain range.~ Courses of Action: 1. The devotees should be discouraged to visit the shrine without having proper warm clothes and other amenities.~ 2. The government should provide warm clothes and shelter to all the devotees visiting the shrine. A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the problem can be solved by warning the devotees of the excessive cold at the shrine. So, only I follow.

question: 34.~ Statement: There has been an unprecedented increase in the number of requests for berths in most of the long distance trains during the current holiday season.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The railway authority should immediately increase the capacity in each of these trains by attaching additional coaches.~ item The people seeking accommodation should be advised to make their travel plan after the holiday. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: People cannot be deprived of going to a certain destination merely for lack of berths. Instead it is the duty of the railway authority to accommodate all the bookings by all means. So, only I follows.

question: 35.~ Statement: The Finance Minister submits his resignation a month before the new budget is to be presented in the Parliament.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The resignation should be accepted and another person should be appointed as the Finance Minister.~ item The resignation should not be accepted. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, an already working Finance Minister shall know better all the plans and resources of the Government and he alone can present a suitable budget. So, course II follows.

question: 36.~ Statement: A large number of people visiting India from country X have been tested positive for carrying viruses of a killer disease.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The government of India should immediately put a complete ban on people coming to India from country X including those Indians who are settled in country X.~ and item The government of India should immediately set up detection centres at all its airports and seaports to identify and quarantine those who are tested positive. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the non-infected persons should not be debarred from visiting India. So, only course II follows.

question: 37.~ Statement: There has been less than forty percent voter turnout in the recent assembly elections.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The election commission should cancel the entire election process as the votes cast are not adequate to represent people.~ item The election commission should take away the voting rs of those who did not exercise their rs. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Re-election would demand repeated expenses and following course II would reduce the voter base permanently. Instead, awareness should be created among the people to use their r to vote effectively. So, neither I nor II follows.

question: 38. Statement: Most of the development plans develop in papers only. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The in-charges should be instructed to supervise the field-work regularly. item The supply of paper to such departments should be cut short. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, proper supervision alone can see the development in p/tice. So, only course I follows.

question: 39.~ Statement: The vegetable traders feel that the prices of onion will again go up shortly in the State 'P' Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!r] item The 'P' State Government should purchase and store sufficient quantity of onion in advance to control prices.~ item The 'F' State Government should make available network of fair price shops for the sale of onions during the period of shortage. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: Clearly, both the courses of action seem appropriate to prevent black-marketing in case of shortage. Hence, both I and II follow.

question: 40. Statement: It is reported that though Vitamin E present in fresh fruits and vegetables is beneficial for human body, capsule Vitamin E does not have the same effect on human body. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The sale of capsule Vitamin E should be banned. item People should be encouraged to take fresh fruits and vegetables to meet the body's requirement of Vitamin E. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The statement implies that capsule Vitamin E does not function so effectively as natural Vitamin E. Since no negative effect of capsule Vitamin E is mentioned, so I does not follow. Hence, only II follows.

question: Statement: Youngsters are often found staring at obscene posters. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1.] item Children should be punished and penalized if they are found doing so. item Any display of such material should be banned. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Bad things att/t more and punishment after the act has been committed is no remedy. The act should be prevented. So, only course II follows.

question: 42. Statement: One of the problems facing the food processing industry is the irregular supply of raw material. The producers of raw material are not getting a reasonable price. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The government should regulate the supply of raw material to other industries also. item The government should announce an att/tive package to ensure regular supply of raw material for food processing industry. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, to remedy the problem of food processing industry, a regular supply of raw material should be ensured. So, course II follows.

question: 43. Statement: The Committee has criticized the Institute for its failure to implement a dozen of regular programmes despite an increase in the staff strength and not drawing up a firm action plan for studies and research. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The broad objectives of the Institute should be redefined to implement a p/tical action plan. item The Institute should give a report on reasons for not having implemented the planned programmes. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The problem is that despite an increase in staff strength, the Institute has failed in its objective of implementing its plan. So, either there should be reasons for the lackening or the plans are a failure and must be revised for p/tical implementation. Thus, both the courses follow.

question: 44. Statement: India has been continuously experiencing military threats from its neighbouring countries. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item India should engage into an all out war to stop the nagging threats. item India should get the neighbours into a serious dialogue to reduce the tension at its borders. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, war is the last resort. First, peaceful talks and negotiations should be indulged in, to settle the issues of dispute. So, only course II follows.

question: 45. Statement: As many as ten coaches of a passenger train have derailed and blocked both pairs of the railway t/ks. Courses of Action:  eginflalign* & 1. The railway authorities should immediately send men and equipment to the spot to clear the railway t/ks.& & 2. All the trains running in both the directions should be diverted to other routes.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The situation demands first diverting other trains to different routes so as to avert any accident, and then clearing the t/ks as soon as possible. Thus, both the courses follow.

question: 46. Statement: There has been a significant drop in the water level of all the lakes supplying water to the city. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The water supply authority should impose a partial cut in supply to tackle the situation. item The government should appeal to all the residents through mass media for minimal use of water. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: The situation can be tackled by periodic cuts in supply, and urging people to conserve water. So both the courses follow.

question: 47.~ Statement: The police department has come under a cloud with recent revelations that at least two senior police officials are suspected to have been involved in the illegal sale of a large quantity of weapons from the state police armoury.~ Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!1] item A thorough investigation should be ordered by the State Government to bring out all those who are involved into the illegal sale of arms. item State police armoury should be kept under Central Government's control. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, the situation demands finding out the real culprits first. So, only I follow.

question: 48. Statement: Financial stringency prevented the State Government from paying salaries to its employees since April this year. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate*[!<arabic*>] item The State Government should immediately curtail the staff strength at least by 30%. item The State Government should reduce wasteful expenditure and arrange to pay the salaries of its employees. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, curtailing of the staff strength will only increase the panic and discontent, and the satisfaction of the employees is a must. So, the Government should arrange for payment of wages. Thus, only course II follows.

question: 49. Statement: The cinema halls are incurring heavy losses these days as people prefer to watch movies in home on TV than to visit cinema halls. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item The cinema halls should be demolished and residential multi-storey buildings should be constructed there. item The cinema halls should be converted into shopping malls. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Doing away with cinema halls is no solution. Hence, none of the courses follows. Instead, certain incentives and promotional schemes should be awarded to cinema hall owners so that they could manage to draw in crowds.

question: 50. Statement: Duty free technology parks where foreign firms can manufacture electronic hardware components are proposed to be established at various places in the country. Courses of Action:  eginenumerate item Government should immediately implement the proposal to augment the foreign currency reserve by exporting the products. item Government should not implement the proposal as it will hinder indigenous production of hardware components. endenumerate A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: The step discussed in the statement would surely give a boost to hardware industry and help our country to stand apart in this field. Thus, only I follows.

question: 1. Statements: In a one day cricket match, the total runs made by a team were 200. Out of these 160 runs were made by spinners. Conclusions:  eginflalign* & i. 80% of the team consists of spinners.& & ii. The opening batsmen were spinners.& endflalign* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: According to the statement, 80% of the total runs were made by spinners. So, I does not follow. Nothing about the opening batsmen is mentioned in the statement. So, II also does not follow.

question: Statements: The old order changed yielding place to new. Conclusions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Change is the law of nature. item Discard old ideas because they are old. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
explanation: Clearly, I directly follows from the given statement. Also, it is mentioned that old ideas are replaced by new ones, as thinking changes with the progressing time. So, II does not follow.

question: 3. Statements: Government has spoiled many top ranking financial institutions by appointing bureaucrats as Directors of these institutions. Conclusions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Government should appoint Directors of the financial institutes taking into consideration the expertise of the person in the area of finance. item The Director of the financial institute should have expertise commensurate with the financial work carried out by the institute. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.
correct_answer: 答案是:E 解析如下: According to the statement, Government has spoiled financial institutions by appointing bureaucrats as Directors. This means that only those persons should be appointed as Directors who are experts in finance and are acquainted with the financial work of the institute. So, both I and II follow.

question: 4. Statements: Population increase coupled with depleting resources is going to be the scenario of many developing countries in days to come. Conclusions:  eginenumerate*[!1] item The population of developing countries will not continue to increase in future. item It will be very difficult for the governments of developing countries to provide its people decent quality of life. endenumerate* A.  B.  C.  D.  E.

question: Statement: Should India encourage exports, when most things are insufficient for internal use itself? Arguments:  eginenumerate*[!1] item Yes.
